observations.title: sdd/indigo-theme/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Migrate to Indigo/Violet Theme

## Intent

The application currently uses a blue/noir color palette (blue-800 primary, amber-500 accent) throughout. The goal is to migrate the entire app to an indigo/violet palette that matches the reports page style, which provides a more modern and cohesive aesthetic. Additionally, custom shadcn/ui component modifications that break the library's defaults need to be reverted, and a theme system should be created for future adaptability.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Update globals.css CSS variables to use indigo/violet palette instead of blue/noir
- Revert shadcn/ui component modifications (button variants, card gradient prop, input focus rings)
- Apply indigo theme to landing page, dashboard, and all other pages
- Create a theme system with CSS variables for future theme switching
- Ensure all UI text remains in Spanish

### Out of Scope
- Adding dark mode variants for the new theme (handled via existing CSS variables)
- Adding additional theme options beyond indigo/violet
- Changing the application's functionality or data flow

## Approach

The migration will follow these steps:
1. Update globals.css with new indigo/violet color palette, maintaining the existing CSS variable structure
2. Revert button.tsx to remove custom variants (gradient, glass, retro)
3. Revert card.tsx to remove custom gradient prop
4. Revert input.tsx to remove hardcoded blue focus rings (use CSS variables)
5. Update landing page hero-section.tsx to use indigo/violet gradients
6. Update dashboard and other pages to use indigo styling
7. Document the theme system in a theme configuration section

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css` | Modified | Primary palette change from blue/noir to indigo/violet |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx` | Modified | Remove gradient, glass, retro variants |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/card.tsx` | Modified | Remove gradient prop |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/input.tsx` | Modified | Remove hardcoded blue focus rings |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/landing/hero-section.tsx` | Modified | Update gradients |
| `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` | Modified | Update retro-grid styling |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/*` | Modified | Update Card gradient usage |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Component styles break after reverting custom variants | Medium | Test each component individually; some variants may need to be preserved if they provide essential functionality |
| Inconsistent styling across pages during transition | Medium | Apply changes in stages, testing each page before moving to the next |
| Focus ring colors not applying correctly | Low | Ensure CSS variables are properly exported and imported in all components |

## Rollback Plan

To revert this change:
1. Restore globals.css from version control (git checkout HEAD -- globals.css)
2. Restore button.tsx, card.tsx, and input.tsx from version control
3. Restore landing page and dashboard components from version control
4. Clear any cached CSS/build artifacts

## Dependencies

- No external dependencies required
- Previous blue-noir-redesign change must be archived (already completed)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] globals.css uses indigo-600 as primary color and violet-500 as accent
- [ ] Button component has no custom variants (gradient, glass, retro removed)
- [ ] Card component has no gradient prop
- [ ] Input component uses CSS variable for focus rings, not hardcoded blue
- [ ] Landing page uses indigo/violet gradients
- [ ] Dashboard uses indigo-styled components
- [ ] Reports page styling is consistent with the new theme
- [ ] All UI text remains in Spanish

observations.title: sdd/indigo-theme/specs
observations.content:
# Delta for Frontend - Indigo/Violet Theme Migration

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: CSS Design Tokens (Color Palette Migration)

The system MUST replace the Blue & Noir color palette with an Indigo/Violet palette:

- Primary color MUST change from `blue-800` to `indigo-600` (#6366f1)
- Accent color MUST change from `amber-500` to `violet-500` (#8b5cf6)
- Light mode gradient background MUST use `from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30`
- Dark mode gradient background MUST use `from-slate-950 via-slate-900 to-indigo-950/20`
- Card borders MUST use `border-slate-200` (light) / `border-slate-800` (dark)
- Card backgrounds MUST use `bg-white/80` (light) / `bg-slate-900/80` (dark)
- Card shadows MUST include `shadow-indigo-500/5` for subtle purple tint
- Section headers MUST use `border-l-4 border-indigo-500`

(Previously: Blue primary (#1e40af), Amber accent (#f59e0b), blue-800/900 gradients)

#### Scenario: Light Mode Gradient Background

- GIVEN the user visits the application in light mode
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN the background displays gradient `from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30`
- AND the gradient provides subtle indigo warmth without being dramatic

#### Scenario: Dark Mode Gradient Background

- GIVEN the user visits the application in dark mode
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN the background displays gradient `from-slate-950 via-slate-900 to-indigo-950/20`
- AND the gradient provides depth with indigo undertones

---

### Requirement: Button Component (Revert Custom Variants)

The system MUST remove custom button variants and revert to shadcn/ui defaults:

- The system MUST NOT support `variant="gradient"` (REMOVED)
- The system MUST NOT support `variant="glass"` (REMOVED)
- The system MUST NOT support `variant="retro"` (REMOVED)
- The system MUST maintain only shadcn/ui default variants: default, secondary, outline, ghost, destructive, link

The system SHALL preserve existing functionality (onClick, disabled, size, etc.).

(Previously: Custom gradient, glass, and retro variants existed)

#### Scenario: Default Button Still Works

- GIVEN a button with default variant
- WHEN rendered
- THEN displays shadcn/ui default styling with indigo-600 background
- AND maintains hover/active states

#### Scenario: Custom Gradient Variant No Longer Exists

- GIVEN a button with `variant="gradient"`
- WHEN rendered
- THEN the button falls back to default shadcn/ui variant
- AND no error is thrown

---

### Requirement: Card Component (Revert Gradient Prop)

The system MUST remove custom gradient functionality:

- The system MUST NOT accept `gradient` prop (REMOVED)
- The card MUST use standard shadcn/ui card styling
- The card SHOULD apply indigo-tinted shadows: `shadow-xl shadow-indigo-500/5`
- Card border MUST use `border-slate-200` (light) / `border-slate-800` (dark)
- Card background MUST use `bg-white/80 backdrop-blur-sm` (light) / `bg-slate-900/80` (dark)

(Previously: `gradient={true}` prop enabled gradient backgrounds)

#### Scenario: Card Default Styling in Light Mode

- GIVEN a Card component with no special props
- WHEN rendered in light mode
- THEN displays `bg-white/80` with `shadow-xl shadow-indigo-500/5`
- AND has subtle `border-slate-200` border

#### Scenario: Card Default Styling in Dark Mode

- GIVEN a Card component with no special props
- WHEN rendered in dark mode
- THEN displays `bg-slate-900/80` with `shadow-xl shadow-indigo-500/5`
- AND has subtle `border-slate-800` border

---

### Requirement: Input Component (Revert Focus Ring)

The system MUST remove hardcoded blue focus rings:

- The system MUST NOT use hardcoded blue focus ring colors
- The focus ring MUST use CSS variable `--ring` which resolves to indigo
- Input focus ring color MUST be `--color-indigo-500` equivalent

(Previously: Hardcoded blue focus rings)

#### Scenario: Input Focus Ring Uses CSS Variable

- GIVEN an Input component
- WHEN the user focuses the input
- THEN displays an indigo-colored focus ring
- AND the ring uses the CSS variable system (not hardcoded)

---

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Theme System Architecture

The system MUST create a theme system using CSS custom properties:

- The system MUST define theme variables under `--theme-*` prefix
- The system MUST define color tokens under `--color-*` prefix that map to Tailwind colors
- The system MUST support theme switching via CSS class on root element
- The system SHOULD allow future theme additions without component changes

#### Scenario: CSS Variables Define Theme Colors

- GIVEN the globals.css
- WHEN parsed by browser
- THEN CSS variables define: `--theme-primary: #6366f1`, `--theme-accent: #8b5cf6`
- AND all components reference these variables

#### Scenario: Future Theme Adaptation

- GIVEN a future theme change request (e.g., emerald theme)
- WHEN the developer updates CSS variables only
- THEN all components automatically reflect the new colors
- AND no component code changes are required

---

### Requirement: Landing Page Hero Section

The landing page hero section MUST use indigo/violet gradients:

- Hero background gradient MUST use `from-indigo-900 via-violet-900 to-slate-900` (dark) or equivalent light variant
- CTA buttons MUST use indigo/violet color scheme
- Text contrast MUST meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 minimum)

#### Scenario: Landing Page Hero with Indigo Theme

- GIVEN the landing page hero section
- WHEN rendered in dark mode
- THEN displays indigo/violet gradient background
- AND CTA buttons use indigo-600 background

---

### Requirement: Reports Page Consistency

The reports page styling MUST remain consistent with the new theme:

- Existing reports page gradients MUST be preserved as they already use indigo/violet
- Section headers MUST use `border-l-4 border-indigo-500`
- All other pages (dashboard, landing) MUST match reports page aesthetic

#### Scenario: Reports Page Matches Theme

- GIVEN the reports page
- WHEN rendered
- THEN styling is consistent with new indigo/violet palette
- AND section headers have indigo left border accent

---

### Requirement: All UI Text in Spanish

The system MUST maintain all UI text in Spanish:

- All labels, buttons, headings, and messages MUST remain in Spanish
- No language changes are part of this migration

#### Scenario: Spanish Text Preserved

- GIVEN any page in the application
- WHEN rendered
- THEN all visible text is in Spanish
- AND no English text appears in the UI

---

## REMOVED Requirements

### Requirement: Blue & Noir Color Tokens (REMOVED)

The system MUST NOT use the previous blue/noir palette:

- Blue primary (#1e40af) is REMOVED
- Amber accent (#f59e0b) is REMOVED
- Blue-800/900 gradients are REMOVED

(Reason: Replaced with indigo/violet palette)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Color Migration

- [ ] `--theme-primary` resolves to `#6366f1` (indigo-500)
- [ ] `--theme-accent` resolves to `#8b5cf6` (violet-500)
- [ ] Light mode background uses `from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30`
- [ ] Dark mode background uses `from-slate-950 via-slate-900 to-indigo-950/20`
- [ ] Card shadows include `shadow-indigo-500/5`

### Component Reverts

- [ ] Button does NOT accept `variant="gradient"`
- [ ] Button does NOT accept `variant="glass"`
- [ ] Button does NOT accept `variant="retro"`
- [ ] Card does NOT accept `gradient` prop
- [ ] Input focus ring uses CSS variable (not hardcoded blue)

### Theme System

- [ ] CSS variables defined under `--theme-*` prefix
- [ ] Components reference theme variables
- [ ] Future theme changes possible via CSS only

### Pages

- [ ] Landing page uses indigo/violet gradients
- [ ] Dashboard uses indigo-styled components
- [ ] Reports page styling consistent with new theme

### Quality

- [ ] All UI text remains in Spanish
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] Responsive design works
- [ ] Focus indicators visible and accessible

observations.title: Design: Indigo/Violet Theme Migration
observations.content:
# Design: Indigo/Violet Theme Migration

## Technical Approach

This change migrates the application from a Blue/Noir palette (blue-800 primary, amber-500 accent) to an Indigo/Violet palette (indigo-600 primary, violet-500 accent), while reverting custom shadcn/ui component modifications and establishing a theme system with `--theme-*` prefixed CSS variables for future adaptability.

The implementation follows a CSS-first approach: all changes are concentrated in `globals.css` with new theme variables, and components are updated to reference these variables instead of hardcoded values. Custom button variants are removed entirely (retro, gradient, glass), the Card gradient prop is removed, and Input focus rings are switched to CSS variables.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: CSS Variable Structure with `--theme-*` Prefix

**Choice**: Create theme variables under `--theme-primary`, `--theme-accent`, `--theme-ring` prefixes, while maintaining backward compatibility with existing `--primary`, `--accent`, `--ring` variables.

**Alternatives considered**: 
- Replace `--primary` directly (breaking change for all components)
- Use Tailwind CSS custom theme config only (doesn't support runtime switching)

**Rationale**: The `--theme-*` prefix allows future theme implementations (emerald, rose, etc.) by simply swapping CSS class on `:root` and redefining variables. This matches shadcn/ui's approach while enabling the "future adaptability" requirement from specs. Backward compatibility is maintained by keeping `--primary` and `--accent` pointing to theme values.

---

### Decision: Remove Custom Button Variants Entirely

**Choice**: Remove `gradient`, `glass`, and `retro` button variants from button.tsx, keeping only shadcn/ui default variants (default, secondary, outline, ghost, destructive, link).

**Alternatives considered**:
- Convert to CSS-only variants using data attributes (still adds complexity)
- Preserve variants but style with indigo (adds maintenance burden)

**Rationale**: The specs explicitly require reverting to shadcn/ui defaults. These custom variants add maintenance burden and break the "reset to defaults" requirement. Any styling needs should use the default variants with existing customization via className or size props.

---

### Decision: Use CSS Variable for Input Focus Ring

**Choice**: Replace hardcoded blue focus ring (`rgba(30,64,175,0.3)`, `rgba(59,130,246,0.2)`) with CSS variable `var(--ring)` that resolves to indigo-500 equivalent.

**Alternatives considered**:
- Use Tailwind's `focus-visible:ring-indigo-500` (requires component rebuild)
- Add `--ring` to Tailwind theme directly (doesn't work with existing component styles)

**Rationale**: The existing focus ring uses hardcoded blue values that bypass the CSS variable system. Changing to `var(--ring)` ensures consistency and allows dark mode to override via the `.dark` selector in globals.css. The ring variable already exists and is properly configured in Tailwind 4.

---

### Decision: Card Shadow with Indigo Tint

**Choice**: Apply `shadow-indigo-500/5` (equivalent to `rgba(99, 102, 241, 0.05)`) to Card shadows instead of removing custom shadows entirely.

**Alternatives considered**:
- Use standard shadow without color tint (loses visual identity)
- Use stronger indigo shadow (too prominent)

**Rationale**: The specs require indigo-tinted shadows. `shadow-indigo-500/5` provides a subtle purple tint that's barely noticeable but creates visual cohesion with the new theme. This is achieved via Tailwind's `shadow-*` utilities that support opacity modifiers.

---

## Data Flow

```
globals.css (CSS Variables)
    │
    ├── :root (Light Mode)
    │   ├── --theme-primary: #6366f1
    │   ├── --theme-accent: #8b5cf6
    │   ├── --ring: #6366f1
    │   └── --primary: var(--theme-primary) [backward compat]
    │
    └── .dark (Dark Mode)
        ├── --theme-primary: #6366f1 (same for consistency)
        ├── --theme-accent: #8b5cf6
        ├── --ring: #818cf8 (indigo-400, brighter for dark)
        └── --primary: var(--theme-primary)

Components (Read CSS Variables)
    │
    ├── Button ──→ Uses --primary (mapped to --theme-primary)
    ├── Input ────→ Uses --ring (CSS variable)
    ├── Card ─────→ Uses shadow-indigo-500/5
    └── Custom ──→ Uses --theme-* for future-proofing
```

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css` | Modify | Replace blue/amber with indigo/violet, add `--theme-*` variables |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx` | Modify | Remove retro, gradient, glass variants |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/card.tsx` | Modify | Remove gradient prop, add indigo shadow |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/ui/input.tsx` | Modify | Replace hardcoded blue focus rings with `var(--ring)` |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/landing/hero-section.tsx` | Modify | Update gradients from blue/amber to indigo/violet |

## Interfaces / Contracts

### CSS Theme Variables (globals.css)

```css
:root {
  /* Theme System - Primary & Accent */
  --theme-primary: #6366f1;    /* indigo-500 */
  --theme-accent: #8b5cf6;    /* violet-500 */
  
  /* Backward Compatibility Aliases */
  --primary: var(--theme-primary);
  --accent: var(--theme-accent);
  
  /* Ring variable for focus states */
  --ring: var(--theme-primary);
}

.dark {
  --ring: #818cf8;  /* indigo-400, brighter for dark mode */
}
```

### Button Variants (button.tsx)

```typescript
// REMOVED variants:
// - variant="gradient"  ❌
// - variant="glass"     ❌
// - variant="retro"     ❌

// AVAILABLE variants (shadcn/ui defaults):
type ButtonVariant = "default" | "secondary" | "outline" | "ghost" | "destructive" | "link";
```

### Card Props (card.tsx)

```typescript
// REMOVED:
// gradient?: boolean  ❌

// CURRENT:
interface CardProps extends React.ComponentProps<"div"> {
  // All standard div props only
}
```

### Input Focus Ring (input.tsx)

```typescript
// BEFORE (hardcoded):
"focus-visible:shadow-[0_0_0_3px_rgba(30,64,175,0.3),0_0_0_5px_rgba(59,130,246,0.2)]"

// AFTER (CSS variable):
// Use Tailwind's built-in focus-visible:ring which references --ring
// Simply remove the hardcoded shadow classes
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Visual | Color palette matches spec (indigo-500, violet-500) | Screenshot comparison, verify hex values in DevTools |
| Visual | Button variants removed (no gradient/glass/retro) | Check button.tsx variants list |
| Visual | Card has no gradient, has indigo shadow | Verify Card renders without gradient prop |
| Visual | Input focus ring uses indigo (not blue) | Focus input, inspect ring color in DevTools |
| Visual | Landing page hero uses indigo/violet gradients | Inspect gradient classes |
| Functional | All existing buttons still work | Click each variant, verify rendering |
| Functional | Spanish text preserved | Visual inspection of all pages |

## Migration / Rollout

No data migration required. This is a pure CSS and component styling change.

**Rollout Plan:**
1. Update `globals.css` with new color variables
2. Update `button.tsx` to remove custom variants
3. Update `card.tsx` to remove gradient prop
4. Update `input.tsx` to use CSS variable for focus ring
5. Update `hero-section.tsx` with new gradient colors
6. Run build and verify no errors
7. Manual visual verification across all pages

**Rollback Plan:**
```bash
git checkout HEAD -- apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css
git checkout HEAD -- apps/frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx
git checkout HEAD -- apps/frontend/src/components/ui/card.tsx
git checkout HEAD -- apps/frontend/src/components/ui/input.tsx
git checkout HEAD -- apps/frontend/src/components/landing/hero-section.tsx
```

## Open Questions

- [ ] Should `--primary` be kept for backward compatibility with existing components, or should we update all components to use `--theme-primary` directly? (Current design maintains alias for safety)
- [ ] Is the indigo-400 ring color (`#818cf8`) for dark mode acceptable, or should it remain indigo-500 (`#6366f1`)? (Design uses 400 for better visibility on dark backgrounds)
- [ ] Do any other pages besides hero-section use the old blue/amber gradients that need updating?

observations.title: Created indigo-theme task breakdown
observations.content:
**What**: Created task breakdown for indigo-theme migration with 17 tasks across 5 phases
**Why**: Orchestrator requested task creation based on proposal, specs, and design artifacts
**Where**: Engram artifact: sdd/indigo-theme/tasks

## Task Structure
- Phase 1: Foundation (globals.css + theme system)
- Phase 2: Core UI Components (button, card, input)
- Phase 3: Glassmorphism Removal (sidebar, sheet, nav-landing)
- Phase 4: Landing Page Updates (hero, features, section-cards)
- Phase 5: Verification

## Key Decisions
- Grouped component reverts by dependency (foundation first)
- Created verification task as final step to match specs acceptance criteria

observations.title: sdd-init/backend
observations.content:
# SDD Context - Backend

**Tech Stack**: FastAPI 0.115+ | SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async) | Alembic | Pydantic v2 | PostgreSQL 16 | pytest | Python 3.14

**Architecture**: Service/UseCase pattern with Clean Architecture principles

**Testing**: pytest con pytest-asyncio y pytest-mock

**Conventions**:
- UUIDv4 primary keys
- created_at/updated_at timestamps on all models
- AsyncSession for all database operations
- One endpoint per file in src/{domain}/api/v1/endpoints/{action}.py
- UseCase for business workflows, Service for CRUD
- Depends() for dependency injection

**Project Structure**:
```
apps/backend/src/
├── {domain}/
│   ├── api/v1/endpoints/   # One file per action
│   ├── application/
│   │   ├── service/        # CRUD logic
│   │   └── usecase/       # Business workflows
│   └── models.py          # SQLAlchemy models
├── shared/                # Cross-domain utilities
└── main.py                # Application entry point
```

**Skills to auto-invoke**:
- fastapi: Creating FastAPI endpoints
- tdd: Fixing bugs, implementing features, refactoring
- pytest: Writing Python tests

**Persistence**: engram (artifact store)

observations.title: sdd/dependency-injection-standardization/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Dependency Injection Standardization

## Intent

El problema actual es que todos los servicios del backend (`UserService`, `StudentService`, `XAPIStatementService`, etc.) crean internamente sus propios repositorios en el constructor en lugar de recibirlos por inyección de dependencias. Por ejemplo:

```python
# Estado actual (PROBLEMÁTICO)
class UserService(BaseService):
    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
        self.db = db
        repository = UserRepository(db)  # ← Crea su propia dependencia
        super().__init__(repository, User)
```

Esto viola el **Dependency Inversion Principle** de SOLID y dificulta el testing porque no podemos inyectar mocks de repositorios fácilmente. El objetivo es estandarizar la inyección de dependencias usando el patrón de FastAPI `Depends()` para que los servicios reciban sus repositorios ya construidos, siguiendo las reglas de Clean Architecture documentadas en `AGENTS.md`.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Modificar todos los servicios que heredan de `BaseService` para recibir el repositorio vía constructor injection
- Crear funciones `get_*_service()` en cada dominio para registrar los proveedores de dependencias (Factory pattern)
- Actualizar los UseCases que inyectan servicios para usar los nuevos providers
- Asegurar que todos los servicios sean testables con mocks de repositorios

### Out of Scope
- Refactorización de repositorios (solo cambiamos cómo se injectan)
- Nuevos features o cambios en lógica de negocio
- Cambios en la base de datos o modelos

## Approach

Se implementará en **3 fases**:

### Fase 1: Refactorizar BaseService y crear patrón Provider
- Modificar `BaseService` para que acepte repositorio por parámetro (ya lo hace correctamente en línea 24)
- Crear módulo de providers en `src/shared/application/providers/` con funciones factory para cada servicio
- Ejemplo:
```python
# src/shared/application/providers/service_providers.py
def get_user_service(
    user_repository: UserRepository = Depends(get_user_repository),
    user_model: User = Depends(get_user_model)
) -> UserService:
    return UserService(repository=user_repository, model=user_model)
```

### Fase 2: Migrar servicios existentes
- UserService, StudentService, ProfessorService, TeacherSettingsService
- XAPIStatementService, FeedbackService, ProgressService, MetricTypeService
- GameService, LevelService, GameInstanceService, SegmentLevelService

Cada servicio pasará de:
```python
def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    repository = XxxRepository(db)
    super().__init__(repository, Model)
```

A:
```python
def __init__(self, repository: XxxRepository, model: Type[Model]):
    super().__init__(repository, model)
```

### Fase 3: Actualizar UseCases y endpoints
- Los UseCases ya usan `Depends()` para inyectar servicios,，只需要 cambiar el provider
- Registrar los nuevos providers en el router de cada dominio

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/shared/application/usecase/base_service.py` | Modificado | Ya está correcto, verificar signature |
| `src/shared/application/providers/` | Nuevo | Factory functions para servicios |
| `src/users/application/service/*.py` | Modificado | 4 servicios |
| `src/statistic/application/service/*.py` | Modificado | 4 servicios |
| `src/game/application/service/*.py` | Modificado | 4 servicios |
| `src/users/application/usecase/*.py` | Modificado | Actualizar Depends |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Romper inyección existente en UseCases | Medium | Tests exhaustivos después de cada cambio |
| Ciclos de dependencia si hay servicios que dependen de otros | Low | Evitar que servicios dependan de otros servicios directamente |
| Performance por usar más objetos en memoria | Low | El overhead es mínimo, solo cambia referencia |

## Rollback Plan

1. Cada cambio será hecho en un commit separado con tag `dependency-injection/`
2. Si algo falla, revertemos el commit específico
3. Mantener backup de la versión anterior de los archivos modificados
4. Los tests deben pasar antes de merge (coverage > 80%)

## Dependencies

- Ninguna dependencia externa requerida
- Solo cambios internos de refactorización

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Todos los servicios reciben repositorio via constructor injection
- [ ] Todos los servicios pueden ser mockeados en tests unitarios
- [ ] Tests existentes pasan sin modificaciones (o con mínimas modificaciones)
- [ ] Coverage de tests se mantiene o mejora
- [ ] No hay breaking changes en la API pública

observations.title: sdd/dependency-injection-standardization/specs
observations.content:
# Delta for Dependency Injection

## Purpose

This specification defines the requirements for standardizing dependency injection in the FastAPI backend using the Factory/Provider pattern. The goal is to enable proper constructor injection of repositories into services, improving testability and adhering to SOLID's Dependency Inversion Principle.

---

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Factory/Provider Pattern for Services

The system SHALL provide a standardized way to create service instances with their dependencies injected via FastAPI's dependency injection system.

#### Scenario: Service provider returns fully-initialized service

- GIVEN a `UserRepository` instance is available in the DI container
- WHEN the function `get_user_service()` is called
- THEN it SHALL return a `UserService` instance with the repository injected via constructor
- AND the service SHALL NOT create its own repository internally

#### Scenario: Provider functions support all service types

- GIVEN the application has multiple service types (UserService, StudentService, ProfessorService, etc.)
- WHEN a provider is defined for each service type
- THEN each provider SHALL accept the corresponding repository as a dependency parameter
- AND the provider SHALL use FastAPI's `Depends()` to declare this dependency

### Requirement: Constructor Injection for Services

All services that inherit from `BaseService` MUST accept their repository as a constructor parameter, not create it internally.

#### Scenario: UserService receives repository via constructor

- GIVEN a `UserRepository` instance and `User` model class
- WHEN `UserService(repository=user_repository, model=User)` is instantiated
- THEN the service SHALL store the repository in `self.repository`
- AND the service SHALL NOT create any repository instance internally

#### Scenario: Service with custom constructor accepts repository

- GIVEN a service that extends `BaseService` and has additional constructor parameters (e.g., `StudentService`)
- WHEN the service is instantiated with all required parameters including the repository
- THEN the repository SHALL be passed to the parent `BaseService` via `super().__init__(repository, model)`
- AND all other parameters SHALL be stored as instance attributes

#### Scenario: Service remains backward compatible with existing UseCases

- GIVEN existing UseCases that use `Depends(UserService)` to inject the service
- WHEN the service is updated to use constructor injection
- THEN FastAPI SHALL automatically resolve the dependency chain (UseCase → Service → Repository → Session)
- AND existing endpoints SHALL continue to function without modification

### Requirement: Repository Provider Registration

The system SHALL provide functions to register repositories in FastAPI's dependency injection system, enabling them to be injected into services.

#### Scenario: Repository provider returns session-based repository

- GIVEN a database session is available in the DI container (via `get_db`)
- WHEN `get_user_repository()` is called
- THEN it SHALL return a `UserRepository` instance initialized with the database session

#### Scenario: Repository providers are used by service providers

- GIVEN `get_user_repository()` is defined as a FastAPI dependency
- WHEN `get_user_service(user_repository=Depends(get_user_repository))` is called
- THEN FastAPI SHALL resolve `get_user_repository()` first
- AND pass the resulting `UserRepository` to `get_user_service()`

### Requirement: Testing Strategy for Services

Services MUST be testable by injecting mock repositories, enabling unit tests that don't require a database.

#### Scenario: Service accepts mock repository in unit test

- GIVEN a mock repository that implements the same interface as `UserRepository`
- WHEN `UserService(repository=mock_repository, model=User)` is instantiated
- THEN the service SHALL use the mock repository for all operations
- AND the test SHALL NOT require any database connection

#### Scenario: UseCase test can inject mock service

- GIVEN a mock `UserService` that returns predefined data
- WHEN `CreateUserUseCase(user_service=mock_service)` is instantiated directly
- THEN the UseCase SHALL use the mock service for its operations
- AND the test SHALL verify UseCase logic without any database or service implementation

---

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Service Constructor Signature

#### Previously:
```python
def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    self.db = db
    repository = XxxRepository(db)  # Creates own dependency
    super().__init__(repository, Model)
```

#### New:
```python
def __init__(self, repository: XxxRepository, model: Type[Model]):
    super().__init__(repository, model)
```

- GIVEN a service that previously created its repository internally
- WHEN the constructor is updated to accept repository as a parameter
- THEN the service SHALL no longer accept `db: AsyncSession` as a parameter
- AND the service SHALL NOT create any repository internally

#### Scenario: Multiple services in the same domain use the same pattern

- GIVEN services: `UserService`, `StudentService`, `ProfessorService` in the `users` domain
- WHEN each service is refactored to use constructor injection
- THEN each service SHALL accept its corresponding repository type as the first parameter
- AND the pattern SHALL be consistent across all services

### Requirement: UseCase Dependency Declaration

#### Previously:
```python
def __init__(self, user_service: UserService = Depends()):
    # Relying on auto-resolution from service's db parameter
```

#### New:
```python
def __init__(self, user_service: UserService = Depends(get_user_service)):
    # Explicit provider reference for clarity and future flexibility
```

- GIVEN a UseCase that depends on a service
- WHEN the UseCase declares the dependency
- THEN it MAY use `Depends(get_xxx_service)` to explicitly reference the provider
- OR it MAY continue to use `Depends()` if FastAPI's auto-resolution is sufficient
- AND the behavior SHALL remain unchanged

---

## REMOVED Requirements

### Requirement: Internal Repository Creation in Services

Services SHALL NOT create their own repositories internally.

- Reason: This violates the Dependency Inversion Principle and makes unit testing difficult because the repository cannot be mocked.

### Requirement: Database Session Injection at Service Level

Services SHALL NOT receive `AsyncSession` directly in their constructor.

- Reason: The service should depend on the repository abstraction, not the database session. The repository handles session management.

---

## Implementation Notes

### Provider Structure

```python
# src/shared/application/providers/service_providers.py
from fastapi import Depends
from src.shared.infrastructure.session import get_db
from src.users.infrastructure.user_repository import UserRepository
from src.users.application.service.user_service import UserService
from src.users.domain.user import User

def get_user_repository(db=Depends(get_db)) -> UserRepository:
    """Provider for UserRepository."""
    return UserRepository(db)

def get_user_service(
    user_repository: UserRepository = Depends(get_user_repository),
) -> UserService:
    """Provider for UserService with injected repository."""
    return UserService(repository=user_repository, model=User)
```

### Service Migration Pattern

Before:
```python
class UserService(BaseService):
    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
        self.db = db
        repository = UserRepository(db)
        super().__init__(repository, User)
```

After:
```python
class UserService(BaseService):
    def __init__(self, repository: UserRepository, model: Type[User] = User):
        super().__init__(repository, model)
```

### Testing Pattern

```python
# Unit test with mock repository
@pytest.fixture
def mock_user_repository():
    return Mock(spec=UserRepository)

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_user(mock_user_repository):
    service = UserService(repository=mock_user_repository, model=User)
    # Test service methods using mock
```

---

## Scenarios Summary

| Category | Scenarios |
|----------|-----------|
| Factory/Provider Pattern | Service provider returns initialized service; Provider supports all service types |
| Constructor Injection | Service receives repository via constructor; Custom constructors remain compatible; Backward compatibility with UseCases |
| Repository Registration | Repository provider returns session-based repository; Repository used by service providers |
| Testing | Service accepts mock repository; UseCase test can inject mock service |
| Modified | Service constructor signature; UseCase dependency declaration |
| Removed | Internal repository creation; Direct session injection |

---

## Next Steps

This specification is ready for the design phase (sdd-design). The design should include:
- Exact file locations for provider modules
- Detailed implementation of each provider function
- Migration order for existing services
- Testing patterns and fixtures
- Backward compatibility verification plan

observations.title: Design: Dependency Injection Standardization
observations.content:
# Design: Dependency Injection Standardization

## Technical Approach

El objetivo es estandarizar la inyección de dependencias en todos los servicios del backend usando el patrón Factory/Provider de FastAPI. El enfoque consiste en:

1. **Crear un módulo de providers** en `src/shared/application/providers/` que exponga funciones factory para cada servicio
2. **Modificar los constructores de servicios** para recibir el repositorio por parámetro en lugar de crearlo internamente
3. **Actualizar los UseCases** para usar los nuevos providers mediante `Depends()`
4. **Mantener compatibilidad hacia atrás** con los endpoints existentes

Este enfoque sigue las reglas de Clean Architecture documentadas en `AGENTS.md` y permite que los servicios sean fácilmente testables mediante la inyección de mocks.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Ubicación del módulo de providers

**Choice**: Crear `src/shared/application/providers/` como módulo centralizado para todos los providers

**Alternatives considered**: 
-分散er providers en cada dominio (`src/users/application/providers/`)
- Crear providers en `src/shared/infrastructure/`

**Rationale**: 
- Un módulo centralizado en `shared` evita duplicación de código
- Los providers son funciones thin que solo instancian servicios, no contienen lógica de negocio
- Es consistente con la ubicación de `BaseService` en `shared/application/usecase/`
- Facilita el mantenimiento: un solo lugar para registrar/actualizar providers

### Decision: Estructura de archivos de providers

**Choice**: Un archivo por dominio (`users_providers.py`, `statistic_providers.py`, `game_providers.py`) + `__init__.py` que exporta todo

**Alternatives considered**:
- Un solo archivo `service_providers.py` con todos los providers
- Un archivo por servicio individual

**Rationale**: 
- Mantiene el código organizado por dominio
- Facilita la migración incremental (un dominio a la vez)
- Evita que un archivo se vuelva demasiado grande
- Es consistente con la estructura existente del proyecto

### Decision: Patrón de nomenclatura para providers

**Choice**: `get_{service_name}_service()` para servicios, `get_{repository_name}_repository()` para repositorios

**Alternatives considered**:
- `create_{service_name}_service()`
- `{service_name}_provider`

**Rationale**: 
- `get_` sigue la convención de FastAPI para dependency injection functions
- Es consistente con `get_db()` ya existente
- Makes it clear these are factory functions, not singletons

### Decision: Modelo de migración

**Choice**: Migración por dominio, de menor a mayor complejidad

**Alternatives considered**:
- Migrar todos los servicios simultáneamente
- Migrar solo los servicios más utilizados primero

**Rationale**:
- Reduce el riesgo al permitir pruebas incrementales
- Permite rollbacks más granulares por dominio
- Es consistente con las 3 fases definidas en el proposal

## Data Flow

### Flujo actual (PROBLEMÁTICO)

```
Endpoint → UseCase → Service → Repository → Database
                     ↓
           [CREA SU PROPIO Repository]
```

### Flujo objetivo (CON DI)

```
Endpoint → UseCase → Provider → Service → Repository → Database
                     ↓
           [RECIBE Repository por parámetro]
```

### Diagrama de dependencias

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      FastAPI DI Container                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  get_db() ──────► AsyncSession                             │
│       │                                                      │
│       ▼                                                      │
│  get_user_repository() ──► UserRepository(db)              │
│       │                                                      │
│       ▼                                                      │
│  get_user_service() ───► UserService(repository=...)       │
│       │                                                      │
│       ▼                                                      │
│  CreateUserUseCase(user_service=Depends(get_user_service))  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## File Changes

### Nuevos archivos a crear

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/shared/application/providers/__init__.py` | Exporta todos los providers |
| `src/shared/application/providers/users_providers.py` | Providers para servicios de users |
| `src/shared/application/providers/statistic_providers.py` | Providers para servicios de statistic |
| `src/shared/application/providers/game_providers.py` | Providers para servicios de game |

### Archivos a modificar

#### Fase 1: Base de providers

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/shared/application/providers/users_providers.py` | Create | Providers para UserService, StudentService, ProfessorService, TeacherSettingsService, LMSCredentialService |
| `src/shared/application/providers/statistic_providers.py` | Create | Providers para XAPIStatementService, FeedbackService, ProgressService, MetricTypeService |
| `src/shared/application/providers/game_providers.py` | Create | Providers para GameService, LevelService, GameInstanceService, SegmentLevelService |

#### Fase 2: Modificar servicios (12 servicios)

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/users/application/service/user_service.py` | Modify | Cambiar constructor de `db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)` a `repository: UserRepository, model: Type[User]` |
| `src/users/application/service/student_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/users/application/service/professor_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/users/application/service/teacher_settings_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/users/application/service/lms_credential_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/statistic/application/service/xapi_statement_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/statistic/application/service/feedback_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/statistic/application/service/progress_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/statistic/application/service/metric_type_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/game/application/service/game_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/game/application/service/level_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/game/application/service/game_instance_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/game/application/service/segment_level_service.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |

#### Fase 3: Actualizar UseCases (ejemplos)

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/users/application/usecase/create_user_usecase.py` | Modify | Cambiar `user_service: UserService = Depends()` a `user_service: UserService = Depends(get_user_service)` |
| `src/users/application/usecase/list_users_usecase.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| `src/auth/application/usecase/register_user_usecase.py` | Modify | Mismo patrón |
| (Todos los UseCases que inyectan servicios) | Modify | Actualizar Depends |

## Interfaces / Contracts

### Provider functions

```python
# src/shared/application/providers/users_providers.py
from fastapi import Depends
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from src.shared.infrastructure.session import get_db
from src.users.infrastructure.user_repository import UserRepository
from src.users.infrastructure.student_repository import StudentRepository
from src.users.infrastructure.professor_repository import ProfessorRepository
from src.users.infrastructure.teacher_settings_repository import TeacherSettingsRepository
from src.users.infrastructure.lms_credential_repository import LMSCredentialRepository
from src.users.application.service.user_service import UserService
from src.users.application.service.student_service import StudentService
from src.users.application.service.professor_service import ProfessorService
from src.users.application.service.teacher_settings_service import TeacherSettingsService
from src.users.application.service.lms_credential_service import LMSCredentialService
from src.users.domain.user import User
from src.users.domain.student import Student
from src.users.domain.professor import Professor
from src.users.domain.teacher_settings import TeacherSettings
from src.users.domain.lms_credential import LMSCredential


# Repository providers
def get_user_repository(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> UserRepository:
    """Provider for UserRepository."""
    return UserRepository(db)


def get_student_repository(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> StudentRepository:
    """Provider for StudentRepository."""
    return StudentRepository(db)


def get_professor_repository(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> ProfessorRepository:
    """Provider for ProfessorRepository."""
    return ProfessorRepository(db)


def get_teacher_settings_repository(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> TeacherSettingsRepository:
    """Provider for TeacherSettingsRepository."""
    return TeacherSettingsRepository(db)


def get_lms_credential_repository(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)) -> LMSCredentialRepository:
    """Provider for LMSCredentialRepository."""
    return LMSCredentialRepository(db)


# Service providers
def get_user_service(
    user_repository: UserRepository = Depends(get_user_repository),
) -> UserService:
    """Provider for UserService with injected repository."""
    return UserService(repository=user_repository, model=User)


def get_student_service(
    student_repository: StudentRepository = Depends(get_student_repository),
) -> StudentService:
    """Provider for StudentService with injected repository."""
    return StudentService(repository=student_repository, model=Student)


def get_professor_service(
    professor_repository: ProfessorRepository = Depends(get_professor_repository),
) -> ProfessorService:
    """Provider for ProfessorService with injected repository."""
    return ProfessorService(repository=professor_repository, model=Professor)


def get_teacher_settings_service(
    teacher_settings_repository: TeacherSettingsRepository = Depends(get_teacher_settings_repository),
) -> TeacherSettingsService:
    """Provider for TeacherSettingsService with injected repository."""
    return TeacherSettingsService(repository=teacher_settings_repository, model=TeacherSettings)


def get_lms_credential_service(
    lms_credential_repository: LMSCredentialRepository = Depends(get_lms_credential_repository),
) -> LMSCredentialService:
    """Provider for LMSCredentialService with injected repository."""
    return LMSCredentialService(repository=lms_credential_repository, model=LMSCredential)
```

### Service constructor pattern

```python
# ANTES (problemático)
class UserService(BaseService):
    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
        self.db = db
        repository = UserRepository(db)  # ← Crea su propia dependencia
        super().__init__(repository, User)


# DESPUÉS (correcto)
class UserService(BaseService):
    def __init__(self, repository: UserRepository, model: Type[User] = User):
        super().__init__(repository, model)
```

### UseCase dependency declaration

```python
# ANTES
class CreateUserUseCase:
    def __init__(
        self,
        db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
        user_service: UserService = Depends(),  # ← Auto-resolución
    ):
        self.db = db
        self.user_service = user_service


# DESPUÉS
class CreateUserUseCase:
    def __init__(
        self,
        user_service: UserService = Depends(get_user_service),  # ← Provider explícito
    ):
        self.user_service = user_service
```

### Exports en __init__.py

```python
# src/shared/application/providers/__init__.py
from src.shared.application.providers.users_providers import (
    get_user_repository,
    get_student_repository,
    get_professor_repository,
    get_teacher_settings_repository,
    get_lms_credential_repository,
    get_user_service,
    get_student_service,
    get_professor_service,
    get_teacher_settings_service,
    get_lms_credential_service,
)

from src.shared.application.providers.statistic_providers import (
    get_xapi_statement_repository,
    get_feedback_repository,
    get_progress_repository,
    get_metric_type_repository,
    get_xapi_statement_service,
    get_feedback_service,
    get_progress_service,
    get_metric_type_service,
)

from src.shared.application.providers.game_providers import (
    get_game_repository,
    get_level_repository,
    get_game_instance_repository,
    get_segment_level_repository,
    get_game_service,
    get_level_service,
    get_game_instance_service,
    get_segment_level_service,
)

__all__ = [
    # Users
    "get_user_repository",
    "get_student_repository",
    "get_professor_repository",
    "get_teacher_settings_repository",
    "get_lms_credential_repository",
    "get_user_service",
    "get_student_service",
    "get_professor_service",
    "get_teacher_settings_service",
    "get_lms_credential_service",
    # Statistic
    "get_xapi_statement_repository",
    "get_feedback_repository",
    "get_progress_repository",
    "get_metric_type_repository",
    "get_xapi_statement_service",
    "get_feedback_service",
    "get_progress_service",
    "get_metric_type_service",
    # Game
    "get_game_repository",
    "get_level_repository",
    "get_game_instance_repository",
    "get_segment_level_repository",
    "get_game_service",
    "get_level_service",
    "get_game_instance_service",
    "get_segment_level_service",
]
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|---------------|----------|
| Unit | Service con mock repository | Instanciar servicio directamente con mock |
| Unit | Provider function | Test que verifica que retorna instancia correcta |
| Integration | UseCase con servicio real | Usar Depends() y base de datos en memoria |
| E2E | Endpoint completo | Test de integración con DB real o testcontainers |

### Unit test para servicio con mock

```python
# tests/users/test_user_service.py
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, AsyncMock
from src.users.application.service.user_service import UserService
from src.users.infrastructure.user_repository import UserRepository
from src.users.domain.user import User


@pytest.fixture
def mock_user_repository():
    """Create a mock UserRepository."""
    return Mock(spec=UserRepository)


@pytest.fixture
def user_service(mock_user_repository):
    """Create UserService with mock repository."""
    return UserService(repository=mock_user_repository, model=User)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_user_calls_repository(user_service, mock_user_repository):
    """Test that create_user calls the repository."""
    # Arrange
    mock_user_repository.create = AsyncMock(return_value=Mock(id=1))
    
    # Act
    await user_service.create({"email": "test@example.com"})
    
    # Assert
    mock_user_repository.create.assert_called_once()
```

### Unit test para provider

```python
# tests/shared/test_providers.py
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch
from src.shared.application.providers.users_providers import (
    get_user_service,
    get_user_repository,
)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_get_user_repository_returns_repository():
    """Test that get_user_repository returns UserRepository instance."""
    # Arrange
    mock_db = Mock()
    
    # Act
    with patch('src.shared.application.providers.users_providers.UserRepository') as mock_repo_class:
        mock_repo_instance = Mock()
        mock_repo_class.return_value = mock_repo_instance
        
        result = get_user_repository(db=mock_db)
        
        # Assert
        mock_repo_class.assert_called_once_with(mock_db)
        assert result == mock_repo_instance


def test_get_user_service_returns_service_with_repository():
    """Test that get_user_service returns UserService with injected repository."""
    # Arrange
    mock_repository = Mock()
    
    # Act
    with patch('src.shared.application.providers.users_providers.UserService') as mock_service_class:
        mock_service_instance = Mock()
        mock_service_class.return_value = mock_service_instance
        
        result = get_user_service(user_repository=mock_repository)
        
        # Assert
        mock_service_class.assert_called_once_with(repository=mock_repository, model=User)
        assert result == mock_service_instance
```

### Fixture de base de datos para integración

```python
# conftest.py
import pytest
import asyncio
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from src.shared.infrastructure.repositories.base_repository import Base


TEST_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite+aiosqlite:///:memory:"


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def event_loop():
    """Create event loop for async tests."""
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop_policy().new_event_loop()
    yield loop
    loop.close()


@pytest.fixture
async def test_db():
    """Create test database session."""
    engine = create_async_engine(TEST_DATABASE_URL, echo=False)
    
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
    
    async_session = sessionmaker(
        engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False
    )
    
    async with async_session() as session:
        yield session
    
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.drop_all)
    
    await engine.dispose()
```

## Migration / Rollout

### Orden de migración (12+ servicios)

**Fase 1: Users domain (5 servicios)**
1. LMSCredentialService (menos dependencias)
2. TeacherSettingsService
3. StudentService
4. ProfessorService
5. UserService (el más usado, migrar último)

**Fase 2: Statistic domain (4 servicios)**
1. MetricTypeService
2. ProgressService
3. FeedbackService
4. XAPIStatementService (más complejo)

**Fase 3: Game domain (4 servicios)**
1. GameService
2. LevelService
3. SegmentLevelService
4. GameInstanceService

### Pasos para cada servicio

1. **Crear provider** en el archivo correspondiente
2. **Modificar constructor** del servicio
3. **Actualizar imports** del servicio (quitar `get_db`)
4. **Actualizar UseCases** que usan ese servicio
5. **Ejecutar tests** para verificar
6. **Commit** con mensaje descriptivo

### Commits sugeridos

```bash
# Fase 1
git commit -m "feat(di): add users_providers.py with factory functions"

git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate LMSCredentialService to constructor injection"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate TeacherSettingsService to constructor injection"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate StudentService to constructor injection"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate ProfessorService to constructor injection"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate UserService to constructor injection"

# Fase 2
git commit -m "feat(di): add statistic_providers.py with factory functions"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate all statistic services to constructor injection"

# Fase 3
git commit -m "feat(di): add game_providers.py with factory functions"
git commit -m "refactor(di): migrate all game services to constructor injection"

# Final
git commit -m "refactor(di): update usecases to use explicit providers"
```

### Backward Compatibility

Los cambios son **breaking** a nivel de constructor de servicios, pero no a nivel de API:

- **API endpoints**: Sin cambios, FastAPI resuelve las dependencias automáticamente
- **UseCases**: Pueden seguir funcionando si no se actualizan (FastAPI auto-resolve)
- **Tests existentes**: Algunos pueden romperse si instancian servicios directamente

Para facilitar la transición:
1. Mantener los providers de forma opcional inicialmente
2. Actualizar UseCases gradualmente
3. Documentar el cambio en el changelog

## Open Questions

- [ ] **¿Necesitamos mantener compatibilidad hacia atrás con el patrón antiguo?** 
  - Los servicios podrían aceptar `db` OR `repository` como parámetro opcional
  - Recommendation: No, migración completa es más limpia

- [ ] **¿Debe el provider ser async?**
  - Los providers de repositorio podrían ser async si la inicialización es costosa
  - Recommendation: No, son funciones sync simples (solo crean instancia)

- [ ] **¿Cómo manejamos servicios con constructores personalizados?**
  - Algunos servicios tienen lógica adicional en `__init__`
  - Recommendation: Mover esa lógica a un método `initialize()` o mantenerla si es necesaria

- [ ] **¿Los tests existentes necesitan actualizarse?**
  - Sí, cualquier test que use `UserService(db=...)` fallará
  - Recommendation: Actualizar en la misma tarea de migración

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| Breaking tests existentes | High | Medium | Actualizar tests en la misma tarea |
| Errores de imports circulares | Medium | High | careful ordering, shared/providers al final |
| UseCases que no se actualizan | Medium | Low | FastAPI auto-resolve funciona, pero menos explícito |
| Olvidar actualizar algún UseCase | Medium | Low | Auditoría de todos los archivos que importan servicios |

observations.title: Phase 3-4 implemented: glassmorphism removal
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented Phase 3 (Glassmorphism Removal) and Phase 4 (Landing Page Updates) for indigo-theme migration

**Why**: Removing removed button/Card variants (gradient, glass, retro) that cause TypeScript errors

**Where**: 
- apps/frontend/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx - removed glassmorphism classes
- apps/frontend/src/components/ui/sheet.tsx - removed glassmorphism from overlay and content
- apps/frontend/src/components/landing/nav-landing.tsx - changed variant="retro" to variant="default", simplified nav backdrop
- apps/frontend/src/components/landing/hero-section.tsx - removed gradient backgrounds, changed button variants, removed gradient prop from Cards
- apps/frontend/src/components/landing/features-section.tsx - removed gradient background, removed gradient prop from Cards

- [ ] 8.3 Verify endpoints still work via integration tests

observations.title: DI Phase 1: Created providers module infrastructure
observations.content:
**What**: Created Phase 1 of dependency injection infrastructure - the providers module with 26 provider functions across Users, Statistic, and Game domains.

**Why**: To enable proper constructor injection of repositories into services, improving testability and adhering to SOLID's Dependency Inversion Principle.

**Where**: 
- `src/shared/application/providers/__init__.py` - exports all providers
- `src/shared/application/providers/users_providers.py` - 10 providers (5 repositories, 5 services)
- `src/shared/application/providers/statistic_providers.py` - 8 providers (4 repositories, 4 services)
- `src/shared/application/providers/game_providers.py` - 8 providers (4 repositories, 4 services)
- `tests/shared/test_providers.py` - test file for providers

**Learned**: 
- The providers follow the NEW pattern (repository injection) but services still use the OLD pattern (db parameter)
- XAPIStatementService has a custom implementation (doesn't inherit from BaseService) and needs special handling in Phase 3
- Services need to be modified in Phases 3-5 to accept the new constructor signature before providers can be fully functional at runtime

observations.title: Phase 6: Updated UseCases to explicit providers
observations.content:
**What**: Updated 7 UseCases to use explicit provider dependencies (Phase 6 of DI standardization)
**Why**: Standardize dependency injection pattern - use explicit `Depends(get_xxx_service)` instead of implicit `Depends()`
**Where**: 
- src/users/application/usecase/create_user_usecase.py
- src/users/application/usecase/update_user_usecase.py
- src/users/application/usecase/delete_user_usecase.py
- src/users/application/usecase/get_user_usecase.py
- src/users/application/usecase/list_users_usecase.py
- src/auth/application/usecase/register_user_usecase.py
- src/auth/application/usecase/change_password_usecase.py
**Learned**: RegisterUserUseCase and ChangePasswordUseCase needed to also inject get_teacher_settings_service. The db dependency was removed from 5 UseCases where it wasn't used. Endpoints still have implicit dependencies but would need UseCase provider functions.

observations.title: Dashboard Style Unification Specs
observations.content:
# Delta for Dashboard UI Styling

## Purpose

This spec defines the unified styling requirements for all dashboard pages to match the premium indigo/violet design language established in the student reports page.

---

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Unified Page Background

All dashboard pages MUST implement the premium gradient background pattern currently used in the student reports page.

#### Scenario: Light mode page background

- GIVEN a user viewing any dashboard page in light mode
- WHEN the page renders
- THEN the background MUST use `bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30`
- AND MUST include a subtle SVG grid pattern overlay with `opacity-[0.03]`

#### Scenario: Dark mode page background

- GIVEN a user viewing any dashboard page in dark mode
- WHEN the page renders
- THEN the background MUST use `dark:from-slate-950 dark:via-slate-900 dark:to-indigo-950/20`
- AND MUST include the same SVG grid pattern overlay

#### Scenario: Background pattern implementation

- GIVEN any dashboard page
- WHEN the page renders
- THEN it MUST include a fixed SVG grid pattern:
  - Pattern definition: `width="40" height="40" patternUnits="userSpaceOnUse"`
  - Path: `M 40 0 L 0 0 0 40` with `strokeWidth="1"`
  - Applied via `rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#grid)"`
  - Container: `fixed inset-0 opacity-[0.03] pointer-events-none`

---

### Requirement: Unified Header Gradient

All page headers MUST use the indigo-to-violet gradient for titles and decorative elements.

#### Scenario: Main page title gradient

- GIVEN any dashboard page with a main h1 title
- WHEN the title renders
- THEN it MUST use `bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-600 to-violet-600` (light mode)
- AND `dark:from-indigo-400 dark:to-violet-400` (dark mode)
- AND MUST include `bg-clip-text text-transparent`

#### Scenario: Header subtitle styling

- GIVEN any dashboard page with a subtitle
- WHEN the subtitle renders
- THEN it MUST use `text-muted-foreground` class

#### Scenario: Decorative header badges

- GIVEN a dashboard page with status badges or decorative badges
- THEN they MUST use:
  - Background: `bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-900/40`
  - Border: `border-indigo-200 dark:border-indigo-800`
  - Text: `text-indigo-700 dark:text-indigo-300`
  - Indicator dot: `bg-indigo-500 animate-pulse`

---

### Requirement: Unified Section Headers

All section headers within dashboard pages MUST follow a consistent styling pattern.

#### Scenario: Section header with icon

- GIVEN a section header with an icon component
- WHEN the section renders
- THEN it MUST include:
  - Container: `relative mb-8 pl-4 border-l-4 border-indigo-500 dark:border-indigo-400`
  - Icon wrapper: `p-2 rounded-lg bg-indigo-100 dark:bg-indigo-900/50 text-indigo-600 dark:text-indigo-400`
  - Title: `text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight`
  - Subtitle (if present): `text-muted-foreground ml-12`

#### Scenario: Section header decorative line

- GIVEN a section header
- THEN it MAY include a decorative gradient line:
  - Element: `absolute bottom-0 left-0 w-0 h-0.5`
  - Classes: `bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 to-violet-500 group-hover:w-full transition-all duration-500`

---

### Requirement: Unified Card and Container Styling

All cards and content containers MUST use the premium glassmorphism style.

#### Scenario: Card container styling

- GIVEN any card or content container on dashboard pages
- THEN it MUST use:
  - Border: `border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-800`
  - Background: `bg-white/80 dark:bg-slate-900/80`
  - Effect: `backdrop-blur-sm`
  - Shadow: `shadow-xl shadow-indigo-500/5`
  - Border radius: `rounded-2xl`

#### Scenario: Card header styling

- GIVEN any card with a header
- THEN the header MUST include:
  - Border bottom: `border-b border-slate-100 dark:border-slate-800`
  - Title: `text-lg font-semibold`
  - Subtitle: `text-sm text-muted-foreground`

---

### Requirement: Unified Interactive Elements

Buttons and interactive elements MUST follow the indigo accent styling.

#### Scenario: Ghost button hover state

- GIVEN a button with variant="ghost"
- WHEN the user hovers over it
- THEN it MUST show:
  - Background: `hover:bg-indigo-50 dark:hover:bg-indigo-950/30`
  - Text: `hover:text-indigo-600`

#### Scenario: Primary action buttons

- GIVEN primary action buttons on dashboard pages
- THEN they SHOULD use the indigo color scheme
- AND MAY include the gradient accent for important CTAs

---

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Page Container Structure

The page container structure MUST be updated from basic layouts to the premium layout.

- PREVIOUSLY: Pages used basic containers like `container mx-auto py-10` or `bg-slate-50`
- NOW: All pages MUST use the full-screen gradient with:
  - Outer: `min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30 dark:from-slate-950 dark:via-slate-900 dark:to-indigo-950/20`
  - Content wrapper: `container mx-auto py-12 px-6 relative z-10`

### Requirement: Header Section Implementation

The header implementation MUST be updated to use gradient backgrounds.

- PREVIOUSLY: Headers used simple solid colors like `bg-gradient-to-r from-primary via-primary to-primary`
- NOW: Headers MUST either:
  - Use the main page gradient background (recommended)
  - OR implement gradient text with `bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-600 to-violet-600 bg-clip-text text-transparent`

---

## REMOVED Requirements

### Requirement: Basic Slate Background

The basic `bg-slate-50` page background is REMOVED.

- Reason: Replaced by the premium gradient background with grid pattern for visual consistency

### Requirement: Simple Primary Header

The simple `from-primary` gradient header is REMOVED.

- Reason: Replaced by indigo-to-violet gradient for premium appearance

### Requirement: Basic Card Styling

Basic card styling without glassmorphism effects is REMOVED.

- Reason: Replaced by cards with backdrop blur and indigo-tinted shadows

---

## Affected Files

The following files MUST be updated to match these specifications:

1. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/metrics/page.tsx`
2. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx`
3. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/help/page.tsx`
4. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/notifications/page.tsx`
5. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/account/page.tsx`
6. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/students/page.tsx`
7. `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/students/[id]/page.tsx`
8. `apps/frontend/src/components/student/student-detail.tsx`
9. `apps/frontend/src/components/student/student-table.tsx`

---

## Design Tokens Reference

### Color Palette
- Primary gradient: `indigo-600` to `violet-600` (light), `indigo-400` to `violet-400` (dark)
- Accent backgrounds: `indigo-100`, `indigo-900/40`, `indigo-900/50`
- Borders: `indigo-500`, `indigo-400`, `indigo-800`
- Shadows: `shadow-indigo-500/5`

### Spacing
- Page padding: `py-12 px-6`
- Section margins: `mb-8`, `mb-12`
- Card padding: `p-6`

### Effects
- Backdrop blur: `backdrop-blur-sm`
- Border radius: `rounded-2xl` for cards, `rounded-lg` for icons
- Transitions: `transition-colors`, `transition-all duration-500`

observations.content: ## Goal Unify dashboard styling across all pages to match the premium indigo/violet theme from students/[id]/reports ## Instructions - Use the indigo gradient pattern: bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-50 via-white to-indigo-50/30 - Header: gradient text from-indigo-600 to-violet-600 with bg-clip-text text-transparent - Add grid pattern SVG overlay with opacity-0.03 - Cards: keep existing Card components but wrap in gradient container - Fix JSX parsing errors by ensuring all wrapper divs are properly closed ## Discoveries - account/page.tsx already had the indigo theme applied (was completed in previous session) - student-table.tsx already had the indigo theme but had a JSX parsing error (missing closing div) - When adding gradient wrapper divs, always add an extra at the end to close the outer wrapper - The pattern from reports page is: min-h-screen gradient + grid pattern overlay + container with relative z-10 ## Accomplished - ✅ Fixed settings/page.tsx - Added gradient background, grid pattern, gradient header - ✅ Fixed student-table.tsx - Fixed JSX parsing error (added missing closing div) - ✅ Fixed student-detail.tsx - Added full indigo/violet theme with gradient header - ✅ All dashboard pages now have consistent indigo/violet premium styling - ✅ Commits made for each change ## Next Steps - None - dashboard style unification is complete ## Relevant Files - apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx - apps/frontend/src/components/student/student-table.tsx - apps/frontend/src/components/student/student-detail.tsx - apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/account/page.tsx (was already done) - apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/help/page.tsx (was already done) - apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/notifications/page.tsx (was already done) - apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/metrics/page.tsx (was already done)

observations.title: Proposal: E2E Integration Between Frontend and Backend
observations.content:
# Proposal: E2E Integration Between Frontend and Backend

## Intent

We need E2E integration testing to catch integration bugs that unit tests miss. The backend and frontend are developed independently with generated API contracts, but we have no guarantee they work together in production-like scenarios. Currently only backend has pytest coverage; frontend has none. Without E2E tests, we risk runtime failures in critical user flows like login, CRUD operations, and game interactions.

## Scope

### In Scope
- **Authentication Flow**: User registration, login with JWT, logout, token refresh
- **CRUD Operations**: Create, read, update, delete for core entities (users, games, levels)
- **Game Interactions**: Start game instance, submit progress, complete level
- **API Contract Validation**: Verify frontend-generated client matches actual backend API
- **Integration Test Setup**: pytest-based integration tests for backend API endpoints
- **E2E Test Setup**: Playwright-based browser tests for critical user journeys

### Out of Scope
- Performance testing and load testing
- Security penetration testing
- Visual regression testing (future consideration)
- Mobile-specific testing
- Cross-browser testing beyond Chrome/Firefox/Safari

## Approach

Implement a dual-layer testing strategy:

1. **Integration Tests (pytest)**: Test backend API endpoints directly using FastAPI TestClient or httpx. These run fast and catch backend contract issues early.

2. **E2E Tests (Playwright)**: Test full user journeys in a real browser against the running application. Covers frontend-backend interaction, UI state, and user flows.

Use the existing Docker Compose dev environment to run tests against real services. Generate Playwright tests using the existing CLI skill reference.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/backend/tests/integration/` | New | Add integration test suite for API endpoints |
| `apps/frontend/e2e/` | New | Add Playwright E2E test suite |
| `apps/frontend/playwright.config.ts` | New | Playwright configuration |
| `infraestructure/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml` | Modified | Add test services if needed (test database) |
| `package.json` (frontend) | Modified | Add Playwright dependencies and scripts |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Flaky tests due to timing issues | Medium | Use Playwright's built-in wait mechanisms, explicit waits for API calls |
| Test data isolation | Medium | Use unique test users per test, cleanup in teardown |
| Environment differences (dev vs prod) | Low | Use Docker Compose for consistent environment |
| Maintaining tests as API evolves | High | Automate contract validation, update tests alongside API changes |

## Rollback Plan

1. Remove `apps/backend/tests/integration/` directory
2. Remove `apps/frontend/e2e/` directory
3. Remove Playwright config and dependencies from frontend
4. Revert any changes to docker-compose files

## Dependencies

- Docker Compose dev environment running (backend, frontend, PostgreSQL)
- OpenAPI contract generation working (existing)
- Backend API stable (no major breaking changes)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Integration tests cover auth, CRUD, and game API endpoints
- [ ] Playwright E2E tests cover login, user dashboard, game flow
- [ ] All tests run in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] Test coverage report shows >80% integration coverage
- [ ] Tests fail fast with clear error messages when API contracts mismatch

observations.title: Explored Docker infrastructure setup
observations.content:
**What**: Explored Docker infrastructure setup for docker-infrastructure-refactor change
**Why**: SDD orchestrator requested exploration of current infrastructure
**Where**: infraestructure/docker/, apps/backend/, apps/frontend/, apps/game/, Makefile

## Current State
- Main docker-compose: infraestructure/docker/docker-compose.dev.yml
- Contains: PostgreSQL 15, Backend (Python 3.11), Frontend (Node 20)
- Ports: 5432 (PostgreSQL), 8000 (Backend), 3000 (Frontend)
- Makefile provides: docker-up, docker-down, docker-logs targets

## Issues Found
1. Duplicate docker-compose in apps/backend/ (conflicting config)
2. Game app has no docker-compose (only Dockerfile for export)
3. Environment variables scattered across apps/backend/.env and apps/frontend/.env
4. infraestructure/docker/.env is empty
5. Inconsistent database credentials between compose files

## Approaches Considered
1. **Consolidate to single docker-compose** — Move all configs to infraestructure/docker/
2. **Use docker-compose extends** — Share common config between compose files
3. **Environment variables centralization** — Single .env in infraestructure/docker/

## Recommendation
Centralize all Docker configuration in infraestructure/docker/ with:
- Single docker-compose.yml for all services
- Centralized .env with all environment variables
- Include game service in docker-compose
