observations.title: Proposal: Crear Endpoint Overview Estadísticas
observations.content:
# Proposal: Crear Endpoint de Overview de Estadísticas

## Intent

Crear el endpoint `/api/v1/statistic/overview` para proporcionar KPIs globales y métricas agregadas del sistema educativo. Este endpoint alimentará la página de dashboard en `/dashboard` con datos reales en lugar de los datos mock actualmente visibles.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Crear schema Pydantic `OverviewKPIs` con métricas: total_students, active_students_this_week, active_students_this_month, total_levels_completed, total_play_time_minutes, average_score
- Crear schema `ActivityOverTime` para evolución temporal de actividad
- Crear schema `LevelPerformanceOverview` para rendimiento por nivel
- Crear schema `OverviewTrends` para tendencias porcentuales
- Crear endpoint GET `/api/v1/statistic/overview` con filtros de fecha opcionales
- Crear UseCase `GetOverviewStatsUseCase` para lógica de agregación
- Integrar con frontend (actualizar hook o crear nuevo para el dashboard)

### Out of Scope
- Reportes por estudiante (ya implementado en sprint anterior)
- Métricas por curso/período académico
- Exportación de reportes (CSV/PDF)

## Approach

Se creará un endpoint RESTful que consulta las tablas de Progress y XAPIStatement para agregar métricas:
- KPIs principales calculados desde ProgressRepository
- Evolución temporal agrupada por día/semana usando funciones de agregación SQL
- Rendimiento por nivel agrupado desde Progress
- Tendencias calculadas comparando períodos actuales vs anteriores

El endpoint aceptará query params opcionales: `start_date`, `end_date`, `period` (7d, 30d, 3m)

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/schemas/overview.py` | New | Schemas Pydantic para overview |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/endpoints/overview.py` | New | Endpoint REST |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/application/usecase/get_overview_stats_usecase.py` | New | Caso de uso |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/infrastructure/progress_repository.py` | Modified | Métodos de agregación |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/router.py` | Modified | Registro del endpoint |
| `apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-dashboard-stats.ts` | New | Hook para obtener datos del overview |
| `apps/frontend/src/types/api.ts` | Modified | Tipos TypeScript |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Queries lentas con muchos datos | Medium | Implementar caché Redis, agregar límites |
| Datos vacíos al inicio | High | Verificar datos seed en DB, manejar gracefully |

## Rollback Plan

1. Revertir cambios en router.py para eliminar endpoint
2. Eliminar archivos creados: schemas, endpoints, useCase
3. Restaurar frontend hook a estado anterior (si se modificó)

## Dependencies

- ProgressRepository existente (debe tener métodos de consulta)
- XAPIStatementRepository para métricas de actividad

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Endpoint responde con código 200 y estructura JSON correcta
- [ ] KPIs muestran valores numéricos razonables
- [ ] Filtros de fecha funcionan correctamente
- [ ] Frontend muestra datos reales en el dashboard
- [ ] Tests unitarios y de integración pasan

observations.title: Specs: Overview Statistics API
observations.content:
# Delta for Overview Statistics API

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Overview Statistics Endpoint

The system MUST provide an endpoint `/api/v1/statistic/overview` that returns aggregated KPIs and metrics for the educational platform dashboard.

#### Scenario: Get overview statistics without date filters

- GIVEN the system has student progress data in the database
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview` without query parameters
- THEN the response MUST return status 200 with JSON containing:
  - `kpis` object with total_students, active_students_this_week, active_students_this_month, total_levels_completed, total_play_time_minutes, average_score
  - `activity_over_time` array with daily activity data for the last 30 days
  - `level_performance` array with performance metrics per level
  - `trends` object with percentage changes vs previous period

#### Scenario: Get overview statistics with date filters

- GIVEN the system has student progress data in the database
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview?start_date=2026-01-01&end_date=2026-03-31`
- THEN the response MUST include only data within the specified date range
- AND all KPIs MUST reflect only the filtered period

#### Scenario: Get overview statistics with period filter

- GIVEN the system has student progress data in the database
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview?period=7d`
- THEN the response MUST return data only for the last 7 days
- AND KPIs MUST reflect only the last 7 days of activity

#### Scenario: Empty database returns zero values

- GIVEN the system has no student progress data
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview`
- THEN the response MUST return status 200
- AND all numeric values in kpis MUST be 0
- AND activity_over_time MUST be an empty array
- AND level_performance MUST be an empty array

### Requirement: OverviewKPIs Schema

The system MUST return a `kpis` object with the following structure:

```json
{
  "total_students": "integer - total unique students in system",
  "active_students_this_week": "integer - students with activity in last 7 days",
  "active_students_this_month": "integer - students with activity in last 30 days",
  "total_levels_completed": "integer - sum of all completed levels",
  "total_play_time_minutes": "integer - total play time in minutes",
  "average_score": "float - average score across all students (0-100)"
}
```

#### Scenario: KPIs calculation accuracy

- GIVEN the database contains 5 students with varying activity
- WHEN overview stats are requested
- THEN total_students MUST equal the count of unique student_ids in progress table
- AND active_students_this_week MUST equal students with at least one progress record in last 7 days

### Requirement: Activity Over Time

The system MUST provide an `activity_over_time` array with daily aggregated metrics.

Each item MUST contain:
```json
{
  "date": "string - ISO date format YYYY-MM-DD",
  "sessions": "integer - number of game sessions",
  "active_students": "integer - unique students who played",
  "play_time_minutes": "integer - total play time"
}
```

#### Scenario: Activity data sorted by date

- GIVEN multiple activity records exist in the database
- WHEN overview is requested
- THEN activity_over_time array MUST be sorted chronologically (oldest first)
- AND MUST NOT include future dates

### Requirement: Level Performance Overview

The system MUST provide a `level_performance` array with aggregated metrics per level.

Each item MUST contain:
```json
{
  "level_name": "string - name of the level",
  "completion_rate": "float - percentage 0-1 of students who completed",
  "average_attempts": "float - average number of attempts",
  "average_time_minutes": "float - average time to complete"
}
```

#### Scenario: Level performance calculation

- GIVEN students have attempted multiple levels
- WHEN overview is requested
- THEN completion_rate for each level MUST be calculated as: (students who completed / total students who attempted) * 100

### Requirement: Trends

The system MUST provide a `trends` object showing percentage changes compared to previous period.

The object MUST contain:
```json
{
  "students_change_percent": "float - change in total students",
  "activity_change_percent": "float - change in activity sessions",
  "score_change_percent": "float - change in average score"
}
```

#### Scenario: Positive trend calculation

- GIVEN current period has 100 sessions, previous period had 80 sessions
- WHEN trends are calculated
- THEN activity_change_percent MUST equal 25.0

### Requirement: Authentication Required

The system MUST require authentication for the overview endpoint.

#### Scenario: Unauthenticated request rejected

- GIVEN no valid authentication token is provided
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview`
- THEN the response MUST return status 401 Unauthorized

### Requirement: Response Caching

The system SHOULD implement caching for overview statistics to improve performance.

#### Scenario: Cached response returned

- GIVEN a request was made within the last 60 seconds
- WHEN the same request is made again
- THEN the response MAY be returned from cache
- AND the cached response MUST reflect the same data as a fresh request

## MODIFIED Requirements

N/A - This is a new endpoint.

## REMOVED Requirements

N/A

## Error Scenarios

### Scenario: Invalid date format

- GIVEN an invalid date parameter is provided
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview?start_date=invalid`
- THEN the response MUST return status 400 Bad Request
- AND the response MUST include an error message describing the invalid parameter

### Scenario: Invalid period value

- GIVEN an invalid period parameter is provided
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview?period=invalid`
- THEN the response MUST return status 400 Bad Request
- AND the response MUST indicate valid period values (7d, 30d, 3m)

### Scenario: End date before start date

- GIVEN start_date is after end_date
- WHEN a GET request is made to `/api/v1/statistic/overview?start_date=2026-03-01&end_date=2026-01-01`
- THEN the response MUST return status 400 Bad Request
- AND the response MUST include an error message about invalid date range

observations.title: Design: Overview Statistics Endpoint
observations.content:
# Design: Overview Statistics Endpoint

## Technical Approach

Se creará un endpoint RESTful `/api/v1/statistic/overview` que consulta las tablas de Progress y XAPIStatement para agregar métricas globales. El patrón sigue la arquitectura existente: Endpoint → UseCase → Repository.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Repository Aggregation Methods

**Choice**: Agregar métodos de agregación en ProgressRepository existente
**Alternatives considered**: Crear un nuevo OverviewRepository separado
**Rationale**: Mantiene la cohesión del dominio statistic. ProgressRepository ya tiene métodos de consulta específicos, agregar agregaciones mantiene el código relacionado juntos.

### Decision: Caching Strategy

**Choice**: Implementar caché en memoria simple con TTL de 60 segundos
**Alternatives considered**: Redis cache, no cache
**Rationale**: Stats overview no requiere datos en tiempo real. Un TTL de 60s reduce carga en base de datos. Redis es overkill para este caso de uso.

### Decision: Date Filter Parameters

**Choice**: Aceptar `start_date`, `end_date` y `period` como query params con validación Pydantic
**Alternatives considered**: Solo period (7d, 30d, 3m), solo fechas específicas
**Rationale**: Flexibilidad máxima. Period es conveniente para casos comunes, fechas específicas para reportesCustom.

### Decision: Response Structure

**Choice**: Estructura anidada con `kpis`, `activity_over_time`, `level_performance`, `trends`
**Alternatives considered**: Arrays planos, múltiples endpoints
**Rationale**: La UI del dashboard espera esta estructura. Reduce el número de requests desde el frontend.

## Data Flow

```
GET /api/v1/statistic/overview?period=7d
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  OverviewEndpoint                           │
│  - Valida query params                      │
│  - Inyecta GetOverviewStatsUseCase          │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  GetOverviewStatsUseCase                    │
│  - calculate_kpis()                         │
│  - calculate_activity_over_time()           │
│  - calculate_level_performance()            │
│  - calculate_trends()                       │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                      │
         ┌────────────┴────────────┐
         ▼                          ▼
┌──────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────┐
│ ProgressRepository│      │ XAPIStatementRepository│
│ - count_students()│      │ - count_sessions()   │
│ - get_active()   │      │ - get_activity_by_date│
│ - aggregate_*()  │      │ - get_trends()        │
└──────────────────┘      └──────────────────────┘
         │                          │
         └────────────┬─────────────┘
                      ▼
         ┌─────────────────────────┐
         │   Database (PostgreSQL) │
         └─────────────────────────┘
```

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/schemas/overview.py` | Create | Schemas Pydantic: OverviewResponse, OverviewKPIs, ActivityOverTimeItem, LevelPerformanceItem, OverviewTrends |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/endpoints/overview.py` | Create | Endpoint GET /overview con query params opcionales |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/application/usecase/get_overview_stats_usecase.py` | Create | Caso de uso con métodos de cálculo de métricas |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/infrastructure/progress_repository.py` | Modify | Agregar métodos: count_students(), get_active_students(), aggregate_kpis(), aggregate_activity_by_date(), aggregate_level_performance() |
| `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/router.py` | Modify | Importar y registrar overview_router |
| `apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-dashboard-stats.ts` | Create | Hook para obtener datos del overview endpoint |
| `apps/frontend/src/types/api.ts` | Modify | Agregar tipos TypeScript para OverviewResponse |

## Interfaces / Contracts

### Schemas Pydantic (Backend)

```python
# overview.py
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Optional
from datetime import date

class OverviewKPIs(BaseModel):
    total_students: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    active_students_this_week: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    active_students_this_month: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    total_levels_completed: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    total_play_time_minutes: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    average_score: float = Field(..., ge=0, le=100)

class ActivityOverTimeItem(BaseModel):
    date: date
    sessions: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    active_students: int = Field(..., ge=0)
    play_time_minutes: int = Field(..., ge=0)

class LevelPerformanceItem(BaseModel):
    level_name: str
    completion_rate: float = Field(..., ge=0, le=1)
    average_attempts: float = Field(..., ge=0)
    average_time_minutes: float = Field(..., ge=0)

class OverviewTrends(BaseModel):
    students_change_percent: float
    activity_change_percent: float
    score_change_percent: float

class OverviewResponse(BaseModel):
    kpis: OverviewKPIs
    activity_over_time: List[ActivityOverTimeItem]
    level_performance: List[LevelPerformanceItem]
    trends: OverviewTrends
```

### Query Params

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Literal

class OverviewQueryParams(BaseModel):
    start_date: Optional[date] = None
    end_date: Optional[date] = None
    period: Optional[Literal["7d", "30d", "3m"]] = None
    
    # Validación: no puede tener both start_date Y period
    # Validación: end_date debe ser >= start_date
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | GetOverviewStatsUseCase.calculate_* methods | Mock ProgressRepository, verificar cálculos |
| Unit | Schemas Pydantic validation | Test serialization/deserialization, edge cases |
| Integration | OverviewEndpoint | TestClient con database in-memory, verificar 200, 401, 400 |
| E2E | Frontend dashboard loading | Playwright: verificar que datos se renderizan |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. Este es un nuevo endpoint que no modifica datos existentes.

## Open Questions

- [ ] ¿Necesitamos filtrar por curso/período académico en el overview? Por ahora no, pero puede ser un feature futuro.
- [ ] ¿El caché debe ser configurable via environment? Por ahora hardcodeado a 60s, considerar hacer configurable si hay problemas de performance.

observations.title: Tasks: Overview Statistics Endpoint
observations.content:
# Tasks: Overview Statistics Endpoint

## Phase 1: Foundation / Schemas Pydantic

- [ ] 1.1 Crear `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/schemas/overview.py` con schema OverviewKPIs — campos: total_students, active_students_this_week, active_students_this_month, total_levels_completed, total_play_time_minutes, average_score
- [ ] 1.2 Crear schema ActivityOverTimeItem en el mismo archivo — campos: date, sessions, active_students, play_time_minutes
- [ ] 1.3 Crear schema LevelPerformanceItem — campos: level_name, completion_rate, average_attempts, average_time_minutes
- [ ] 1.4 Crear schema OverviewTrends — campos: students_change_percent, activity_change_percent, score_change_percent
- [ ] 1.5 Crear schema OverviewResponse que contiene los 4 schemas anteriores
- [ ] 1.6 Crear schema OverviewQueryParams con validación — start_date (optional date), end_date (optional date), period (optional Literal["7d", "30d", "3m"])

## Phase 2: Repository Extensions

- [ ] 2.1 Agregar método `count_students()` a ProgressRepository — retorna count de estudiantes únicos
- [ ] 2.2 Agregar método `get_active_students(days: int)` — retorna estudiantes con actividad en los últimos N días
- [ ] 2.3 Agregar método `aggregate_kpis(start_date, end_date)` — calcula total_levels_completed, total_play_time_minutes, average_score
- [ ] 2.4 Agregar método `aggregate_activity_by_date(start_date, end_date)` — agrupa sesiones por fecha
- [ ] 2.5 Agregar método `aggregate_level_performance()` — agrupa por level con completion_rate, average_attempts, average_time_minutes

## Phase 3: UseCase Implementation

- [ ] 3.1 Crear `apps/backend/src/statistic/application/usecase/get_overview_stats_usecase.py` — clase GetOverviewStatsUseCase
- [ ] 3.2 Implementar método `calculate_kpis(start_date, end_date)` — obtiene métricas desde ProgressRepository
- [ ] 3.3 Implementar método `calculate_activity_over_time(start_date, end_date)` — obtiene evolución temporal
- [ ] 3.4 Implementar método `calculate_level_performance()` — obtiene rendimiento por nivel
- [ ] 3.5 Implementar método `calculate_trends(current_period, previous_period)` — calcula cambios porcentuales
- [ ] 3.6 Implementar método `execute(query_params)` — método principal que orquesta todos los cálculos y retorna OverviewResponse

## Phase 4: Endpoint Creation

- [ ] 4.1 Crear `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/endpoints/overview.py` — clase OverviewEndpoint
- [ ] 4.2 Configurar GET /overview con query params opcionales
- [ ] 4.3 Integrar GetOverviewStatsUseCase via Depends()
- [ ] 4.4 Implementar validación de query params (start_date vs period mutual exclusion)
- [ ] 4.5 Implementar manejo de errores (400 para params inválidos, 401 para no autenticado)
- [ ] 4.6 Agregar caché en memoria con TTL 60s

## Phase 5: Router Integration

- [ ] 5.1 Importar overview_router en `apps/backend/src/statistic/api/v1/router.py`
- [ ] 5.2 Registrar router con router.include_router(overview_router)

## Phase 6: Frontend Integration

- [ ] 6.1 Crear `apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-dashboard-stats.ts` — hook para obtener datos del overview
- [ ] 6.2 Agregar tipos TypeScript en `apps/frontend/src/types/api.ts` para OverviewResponse
- [ ] 6.3 Conectar hook con la UI del dashboard en `/dashboard`

## Phase 7: Testing

- [ ] 7.1 Escribir tests unitarios para GetOverviewStatsUseCase — mock ProgressRepository, verificar cálculo de KPIs
- [ ] 7.2 Escribir tests de validación de schemas Pydantic — test serialization/deserialization con datos válidos e inválidos
- [ ] 7.3 Escribir tests de integración para endpoint HTTP con TestClient — verificar response 200, 401, 400
- [ ] 7.4 Escribir test E2E con Playwright — verificar que el dashboard carga datos reales

## Phase 8: Cleanup

- [ ] 8.1 Verificar que el código cumpla con lint/typecheck del proyecto
- [ ] 8.2 Eliminar comments o código mock innecesario
- [ ] 8.3 Verificar que todos los tests pasan

## Implementation Order

1. Primero schemas (Phase 1) — sin dependencias, base de todo
2. Repository (Phase 2) — los useCases dependende de estos métodos
3. UseCase (Phase 3) — necesita los métodos del repository
4. Endpoint (Phase 4) — necesita el UseCase
5. Router (Phase 5) — integra el endpoint
6. Frontend (Phase 6) — consume el endpoint
7. Testing (Phase 7) — verifica todo
8. Cleanup (Phase 8) — pulido final

observations.title: SDD Completed: Overview Statistics Endpoint
observations.content:
# SDD Workflow Completado: Overview Statistics Endpoint

## Resumen
Se implementó el endpoint `/api/v1/statistic/overview` con KPIs globales para el dashboard educativo.

## Commits Realizados
```
e8eaeb7 feat(statistic): add overview schemas for dashboard KPIs
8dcba80 feat(statistic): add aggregation methods to ProgressRepository
9846ada feat(statistic): add GetOverviewStatsUseCase for dashboard KPIs
51641f0 feat(statistic): add overview endpoint and register in router
d9cd4c9 feat(statistic): register overview router in main router
ec8b919 feat(frontend): add dashboard stats hook and TypeScript types
732de32 feat(statistic): add overview endpoint tests and fix Pydantic date conflicts
```

## Archivos Creados/Modificados
- Backend: overview.py (schemas), overview.py (endpoint), get_overview_stats_usecase.py, progress_repository.py, router.py
- Frontend: use-dashboard-stats.ts, api.ts
- Tests: test_overview_schemas.py, test_get_overview_stats_usecase.py, test_overview_endpoint.py

## Endpoint
- **GET** `/api/v1/statistic/overview`
- Query params: `start_date`, `end_date`, `period` (7d, 30d, 3m)
- Requiere autenticación HTTPBearer

observations.title: Proposal: Integrar Overview con Dashboard
observations.content:
# Proposal: Integrar Overview Endpoint con Dashboard Frontend

## Intent

Conectar el dashboard `/dashboard` con datos reales del endpoint `/api/v1/statistic/overview`. Actualmente la página muestra datos hardcoded/mock. El hook `useDashboardStats` ya existe pero no se utiliza.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Modificar `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` para usar el hook `useDashboardStats`
- Modificar `SectionCards` para aceptar datos por props en lugar de datos hardcoded
- Modificar `ChartAreaInteractive` para aceptar datos por props
- Manejar estados de carga (loading) y error en la UI
- Verificar que la autenticación funcione correctamente

### Out of Scope
- Crear nuevos componentes (usar los existentes modificándolos)
- Tests E2E completos
- Eliminar archivos de datos mock existentes (solo limpiar si es necesario)

## Approach

**Reemplazo incremental** — Modificar componentes existentes para aceptar datos por props. El dashboard usa "use client" y llama al hook, pasando los datos a los componentes hijos.

1. Dashboard page.tsx importa y usa useDashboardStats
2. SectionCards acepta props (kpis data)
3. ChartAreaInteractive acepta props (activity data)
4. Estados de loading/error manejados

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` | Modified | Importar y usar useDashboardStats hook |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/section-cards.tsx` | Modified | Aceptar props de KPIs |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/chart-area-interactive.tsx` | Modified | Aceptar props de activity data |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Autenticación falla (401) | Medium | Verificar que el token se envíe correctamente |
| Datos vacíos al inicio | High | Mostrar skeleton/loading state |
| Tipos incompatibles | Low | Verificar mapping snake_case → camelCase |

## Rollback Plan

1. Revertir cambios en page.tsx
2. Restaurar datos hardcoded en SectionCards y ChartAreaInteractive
3. Eliminar uso del hook

## Dependencies

- Endpoint `/api/v1/statistic/overview` debe estar corriendo
- Hook `useDashboardStats` ya existe y funciona
- Autenticación configurada en el cliente

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Dashboard muestra datos reales del endpoint
- [ ] Loading state se muestra mientras cargan datos
- [ ] Error state se muestra si falla la petición
- [ ] KPIs (tarjetas superiores) muestran valores correctos
- [ ] Gráfico de actividad muestra datos reales
- [ ]Cambios de período (7d/30d/3m) actualizan los datos

observations.title: Specs: Integrar Overview con Dashboard
observations.content:
# Delta for Dashboard Frontend Integration

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: Dashboard Page Data Source

The dashboard page MUST fetch data from the `/api/v1/statistic/overview` endpoint using the `useDashboardStats` hook instead of using hardcoded data.

(Previously: Dashboard used hardcoded data in components)

#### Scenario: Dashboard loads with real data

- GIVEN the user navigates to /dashboard
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN the useDashboardStats hook SHOULD be called
- AND data from the API SHOULD be displayed in the KPIs cards
- AND activity data SHOULD be displayed in the chart

#### Scenario: Dashboard shows loading state

- GIVEN the user navigates to /dashboard
- WHEN the data is still loading
- THEN a loading skeleton or spinner SHOULD be displayed
- AND the page SHOULD NOT show hardcoded fallback data

#### Scenario: Dashboard shows error state

- GIVEN the user navigates to /dashboard
- WHEN the API request fails (401, 500, network error)
- THEN an error message SHOULD be displayed
- AND the user SHOULD be able to retry loading the data

### Requirement: SectionCards Component Data Input

The SectionCards component MUST accept KPIs data via props instead of using internal hardcoded values.

(Previously: SectionCards had hardcoded card data)

#### Scenario: SectionCards receives KPIs data

- GIVEN the SectionCards component receives kpis prop
- WHEN rendering
- THEN each card SHOULD display the corresponding value from the kpis object
- AND the change percentage indicator SHOULD be shown

#### Scenario: SectionCards receives null data

- GIVEN the SectionCards component receives null or undefined kpis
- WHEN rendering
- THEN a loading skeleton SHOULD be displayed for each card

### Requirement: ChartAreaInteractive Component Data Input

The ChartAreaInteractive component MUST accept activity data via props instead of using internal hardcoded values.

(Previously: ChartAreaInteractive had 90+ days of fake data)

#### Scenario: Chart receives activity data

- GIVEN the ChartAreaInteractive receives activityOverTime prop
- WHEN rendering
- THEN the chart SHOULD display the data points from the prop
- AND the chart SHOULD update when data changes

#### Scenario: Chart receives empty data

- GIVEN the ChartAreaInteractive receives empty array
- WHEN rendering
- THEN an empty state message SHOULD be displayed
- OR a flat line at zero SHOULD be shown

### Requirement: Period Filter

The dashboard MUST support switching between time periods (7d, 30d, 3m) which updates the API request.

#### Scenario: User changes period filter

- GIVEN the user clicks on a period filter (7d, 30d, or 3m)
- WHEN the filter changes
- THEN the useDashboardStats hook SHOULD be called with the new period
- AND the displayed data SHOULD update to reflect the new period

#### Scenario: Period filter default

- GIVEN the user navigates to /dashboard without selecting a period
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN the default period of "30d" SHOULD be used

### Requirement: Authentication

The dashboard API requests MUST include proper authentication tokens.

#### Scenario: Authenticated request

- GIVEN the user is logged in with a valid token
- WHEN the dashboard loads
- THEN the API request SHOULD include the Bearer token
- AND the response SHOULD return 200 with data

#### Scenario: Unauthenticated request

- GIVEN the user is not logged in or token is invalid
- WHEN the dashboard loads
- THEN the API request SHOULD return 401
- AND an appropriate error message SHOULD be displayed

observations.title: Design: Integrar Overview con Dashboard
observations.content:
# Design: Integrar Overview Endpoint con Dashboard

## Technical Approach

Modificar el dashboard para usar el hook `useDashboardStats` existente y pasar los datos a los componentes `SectionCards` y `ChartAreaInteractive` mediante props. Los componentes se modifican para aceptar datos opcionales, con fallback a datos hardcoded durante la carga.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Componentes controlados por props vs nuevos componentes

**Choice**: Modificar componentes existentes para aceptar props opcionales
**Alternatives considered**: Crear nuevos componentes separados (DashboardKPIs, ActivityChart)
**Rationale**: Mantiene la estructura del proyecto limpia, menos código duplicado, los componentes existentes ya tienen todo el styling listo

### Decision: Loading state con skeleton vs spinner

**Choice**: Usar skeleton en las tarjetas y gráfico
**Alternatives considered**: Spinner simple, texto de "cargando"
**Rationale**: Mejor UX - el usuario ve la estructura de la página mientras carga, más moderno

### Decision: Manejo de errores

**Choice**: Mostrar mensaje de error con botón de reintentar
**Alternatives considered**: Mostrar datos en caché, silently fail
**Rationale**: UX clara cuando hay error, el usuario sabe qué pasó y puede reintentar

## Data Flow

```
/dashboard page.tsx (use client)
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  useDashboardStats(period)              │
│  - Fetch /api/v1/statistic/overview      │
│  - Returns: kpis, activityOverTime,      │
│             levelPerformance, trends    │
└─────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                  │
         ┌────────┴────────┐
         ▼                 ▼
   ┌──────────┐      ┌────────────────┐
   │KPIs Data │      │Activity Data   │
   └──────────┘      └────────────────┘
         │                 │
         ▼                 ▼
┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐
│ SectionCards    │  │ChartAreaInteractive
│ (with props)   │  │ (with props)    │
└─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘
```

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` | Modify | Importar useDashboardStats, agregar "use client", pasar datos a componentes |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/section-cards.tsx` | Modify | Agregar props opcionales para datos de KPIs |
| `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/chart-area-interactive.tsx` | Modify | Agregar props para datos de actividad, aceptar período |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```typescript
// Dashboard page.tsx - uso del hook
"use client"
import { useDashboardStats } from "@/hooks/use-dashboard-stats"

export default function DashboardPage() {
  const { kpis, activityOverTime, isLoading, error, refetch } = useDashboardStats("30d")
  // pasar datos a SectionCards y ChartAreaInteractive
}

// SectionCards - props
interface SectionCardsProps {
  kpis?: {
    totalStudents: number
    activeStudentsThisWeek: number
    activeStudentsThisMonth: number
    totalLevelsCompleted: number
    totalPlayTimeMinutes: number
    averageScore: number
  } | null
  trends?: {
    studentsChangePercent: number
    activityChangePercent: number
    scoreChangePercent: number
  } | null
  isLoading?: boolean
}

// ChartAreaInteractive - props
interface ChartAreaInteractiveProps {
  data?: Array<{
    date: string
    sessions: number
    activeStudents: number
    playTimeMinutes: number
  }>
  period?: "7d" | "30d" | "3m"
  onPeriodChange?: (period: string) => void
  isLoading?: boolean
}
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|----------|
| Unit | Hook useDashboardStats | Verificar que hace fetch correcto |
| Integration | Dashboard render con datos | Verificar que componentes reciben props |
| E2E | Navigación a /dashboard | Playwright: verificar datos reales |

## Migration / Rollout

No migration required. Es un cambio gradual que mantiene backward compatibility.

## Open Questions

- [ ] ¿El endpoint requiere autenticación? Verificar que el hook envíe el token correctamente
- [ ] ¿Qué pasa si no hay datos en la DB? Verificar que se muestre empty state vs skeleton

observations.title: Tasks: Integrar Overview con Dashboard
observations.content:
# Tasks: Integrar Overview Endpoint con Dashboard

## Phase 1: Modify SectionCards Component

- [ ] 1.1 Agregar tipos TypeScript para las props de SectionCards en `apps/frontend/src/components/dashboard/section-cards.tsx` — interfaz con kpis, trends, isLoading
- [ ] 1.2 Modificar SectionCards para aceptar props opcionales — si recibe datos los usa, si no usa fallback hardcoded
- [ ] 1.3 Agregar skeleton/loading state cuando isLoading es true

## Phase 2: Modify ChartAreaInteractive Component

- [ ] 2.1 Agregar tipos TypeScript para las props de ChartAreaInteractive — interfaz con data, period, onPeriodChange, isLoading
- [ ] 2.2 Modificar ChartAreaInteractive para aceptar datos por props — usar props data en lugar de hardcoded chartData
- [ ] 2.3 Agregar filtro de período (7d/30d/3m) que llama a onPeriodChange
- [ ] 2.4 Agregar skeleton/loading state cuando isLoading es true

## Phase 3: Modify Dashboard Page

- [ ] 3.1 Agregar "use client" al inicio de `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`
- [ ] 3.2 Importar useDashboardStats de `@/hooks/use-dashboard-stats`
- [ ] 3.3 Agregar estado para período seleccionado (useState)
- [ ] 3.4 Llamar useDashboardStats con el período seleccionado
- [ ] 3.5 Pasar datos (kpis, trends, activityOverTime, isLoading, error) a SectionCards y ChartAreaInteractive via props

## Phase 4: Handle Error States

- [ ] 4.1 Agregar manejo de error en dashboard — si error existe, mostrar mensaje con botón de reintentar
- [ ] 4.2 Crear componente de error state o agregar en dashboard page
- [ ] 4.3 Implementar función refetch en el botón de reintentar

## Phase 5: Verify Integration

- [ ] 5.1 Verificar que el endpoint funciona con autenticación — revisar que el hook envía el token
- [ ] 5.2 Probar cambios de período — verificar que los datos se actualizan
- [ ] 5.3 Verificar que no hay errores de TypeScript

## Implementation Order

1. Primero SectionCards (Phase 1) — componentes hijos primero
2. Luego ChartAreaInteractive (Phase 2) — tiene más lógica de período
3. Luego dashboard page (Phase 3) — conecta todo
4. Error states (Phase 4) — después de lo básico
5. Verificación (Phase 5) — al final

## Notes

- El hook useDashboardStats ya hace el mapping de snake_case → camelCase
- Los componentes deben mantener backward compatibility (aceptar datos o fallback)
- El período por defecto es "30d"

observations.title: Agregados ParrafosFiscales hardcodeados en FACEL
observations.content:
**What**: Agregué los ParrafosFiscales hardcodeados en el mapper de DetallesFactura, según el ejemplo XML proporcionado por el usuario.

**Why**: Los ParrafosFiscales son requeridos en el XML de FACEL. El ejemplo tiene códigos "0114822" y "0130000".

**Where**: `/apps/cuba/cuba/cuba_integrations/integrations/facel/facel_service.py` - método `_map_detalles_factura`

**Learned**: 
- El ejemplo FACEL NO tiene CodigoRegistroONEI, CuentaBancaria es opcional, NoInscripcionRegistroComercial usa formato "A70104" (letra + 5 dígitos)
- Validaciones anteriores eran muy estrictas - aflojé las validaciones para estos campos

observations.title: Usuario quiere usar SDD para cambio en FACEL
observations.content:
**What**: El usuario quiere usar SDD para implementar un cambio en la integración FACEL

**Why**: Necesita agregar datos personales del proveedor (nombre, apellido, CI) basándose en el usuario que creó la factura (owner), uniendo con la tabla Employee

**Where**: apps/cuba/cuba/cuba_integrations/integrations/facel/facel_service.py

**Learned**: El usuario quiere seguir la metodología SDD para este cambio

observations.title: Implementado facel-owner-employee-data
observations.content:
**What**: Se modificó el método `_map_proveedor` en facel_service.py para obtener los datos personales del representante legal desde el Employee vinculado al owner del Sales Invoice.

**Why**: La integración FACEL requiere los datos personales (nombre, apellido, CI) del proveedor/empresa, que deben tomarse del usuario owner de la factura y su registro Employee asociado.

**Where**: apps/cuba/cuba/cuba_integrations/integrations/facel/facel_service.py - método `_map_proveedor`, líneas 255-283

**Learned**:
- Se usa `sales_invoice.get("owner")` para obtener el usuario owner
- `frappe.db.get_value` con `as_dict=True` devuelve un objeto con los campos solicitados
- El fallback a Company se mantiene si no existe Employee

observations.title: Fixed 4 bugs in statistic/overview

observations.title: Appearance Settings Sync - Proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Appearance Settings Sync

## Intent

El sistema de apariencia tiene la UI y el backend funcionando, pero los settings no se sincronizan correctamente con la aplicación. Específicamente: color_theme se guarda en DB pero no se aplica al CSS, animations_enabled no controla las animaciones, y hay flash de theme al cargar. Necesitamos una sincronización completa que permita al profesor personalizar y ver sus preferencias reflejadas en tiempo real.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Sincronizar selección de dark mode con backend (ya funciona, asegurar persistencia)
- Sincronizar selección de color theme al CSS dinámicamente (aplicar --theme-primary basado en selección)
- Sincronizar toggles de animaciones para deshabilitar realmente animaciones CSS
- Eliminar theme flash en load con hydration apropiado

### Out of Scope
- Nuevos temas de color (solo现有 6 temas)
- Animaciones complejas adicionales
- Tema por usuario individual (solo profesor)

## Approach

Implementar un ThemeProvider wrapper que:
1. Use useEffect para cargar settings desde API al montar
2. Aplique color themedinámicamente injectando CSS variables en :root
3. Provea un context para animations_enabled que desactive transiciones globally
4. Use forcedTheme en next-themes para prevenir flash

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx` | Modified | Ya funciona, asegurar sync |
| `apps/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` | Modified | Agregar ThemeProvider wrapper |
| `apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css` | Modified | Usar CSS variables en vez de hardcoded |
| Nuevo: `apps/frontend/src/theme-provider.tsx` | New | Provider wrapper con sync logic |
| Nuevo: `apps/frontend/src/animations-context.tsx` | New | Context para toggle animaciones |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Flash aún persiste | Medium | Forzar forcedTheme en next-themes |
| Race condition en API call | Low | Mostrar loading state hasta queThemeProvider cargue |
| Slow performance con API call en client | Medium | Cachear en localStorage como fallback |

## Rollback Plan

1. Revertir cambios en layout.tsx (remover ThemeProvider)
2. Revertir globals.css (volver a hardcoded --theme-primary)
3. Mantener backend sin cambios (ya funciona)

## Dependencies

- Backend endpoint GET /api/v1/users/professors/settings debe estaroperational (ya está)
- next-themes package instalado (ya está)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Selecting color theme apply --theme-primary dinámicamente
- [ ] Toggling animations deshabilita todas las transiciones
- [ ] No hay flash de theme al refresh
- [ ] Settings persisten después de logout/login

observations.title: Appearance Settings Sync - Specs
observations.content:
# Delta Spec: Appearance Settings Sync

## Purpose

This spec describes the CHANGES needed to synchronize appearance settings (color theme, animations) from the backend with the frontend UI and CSS. Currently settings are saved but never applied to the application.

## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Color Theme Dynamic Application

The system MUST apply the saved color theme to CSS dynamically when the user selects or changes a color theme in settings.

The system SHALL inject CSS custom properties (--theme-primary, --theme-accent, --theme-ring) into :root based on the selected color theme from backend settings.

The system SHOULD maintain the mapping between color theme names and their hex values.

#### Scenario: User selects color theme in settings

- GIVEN the user is on the settings page with color theme selection UI
- WHEN the user clicks a color theme button (e.g., "Esmeralda")
- THEN the system SHOULD update the settings.animationsEnabled value in state
- AND the system SHOULD persist the change to backend via API

#### Scenario: Page loads with stored color theme

- GIVEN the user has previously saved "Violeta" as their color theme
- AND the user opens the application in a new session
- WHEN the page loads and ThemeProvider initializes
- THEN the system SHOULD apply --theme-primary: #8b5cf6 (violet-500) to :root
- AND all components using --theme-primary SHOULD reflect violet

### Requirement: Animations Toggle

The system MUST disable all CSS transitions and animations when the user disables animations in settings.

The system SHALL add a CSS rule that sets all transition and animation durations to 0s when animations are disabled.

The system SHOULD provide immediate feedback in the UI when toggling animations.

#### Scenario: User toggles animations off

- GIVEN the user is on the settings page with animations toggle
- WHEN the user turns off the animations switch
- THEN the system SHOULD set --animation-duration: 0s globally
- AND all transitions SHOULD complete instantly (0ms)

#### Scenario: Page loads with animations disabled

- GIVEN the user has previously disabled animations
- AND the user opens the application in a new session
- WHEN the page loads
- THEN all elements SHOULD have no visible transitions
- AND any loading spinners SHOULD be static or hidden

### Requirement: Theme Flash Prevention

The system MUST prevent theme flash (FOUC) when loading the application.

The system SHALL use forcedTheme in next-themes to bypass system theme detection.

The system SHOULD wait for settings to load before rendering to ensure correct theme application.

#### Scenario: User navigates to application

- GIVEN the user has dark mode as their saved theme preference
- AND the user navigates to any page
- WHEN the page begins rendering
- THEN the system SHOULD render with dark mode immediately
- AND the user SHOULD NOT see a light mode flash

## MODIFIED Requirements

### Requirement: ThemeProvider Configuration

**Original**: ThemeProvider uses defaultTheme="system" and enableSystem

**New**: ThemeProvider MUST:
- Accept explicit theme value from props OR from localStorage/cache
- Use forcedTheme when the user has an explicit preference (not "system")
- NOT use system as default to prevent flash

(The current ThemeProvider.tsx only wraps next-themes without custom logic. Needs modification or a new wrapper.)

### Requirement: Settings Page API Integration

**Original**: Settings page reads settings via fetch but only theme is synced to ThemeToggle

**New**: The settings page SHOULD pass colorTheme and animationsEnabled to a global context or provider that applies these settings to the application.

(Currently: settings-content.tsx has the UI but no connection to CSS variables. Needs integration with ThemeProvider or new context.)

## REMOVED Requirements

### Requirement: Hardcoded Theme Colors in CSS

The system SHOULD NOT hardcode --theme-primary in globals.css as #6366f1 permanently.

Instead: --theme-primary SHOULD reference a CSS variable that can be overridden.

## Color Theme Mapping

| Name   | Primary (--theme-primary) | Accent (--theme-accent) |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Indigo | #6366f1             | #8b5cf6           |
| Violeta | #8b5cf6            | #a855f7            |
| Esmeralda | #10b981          | #14b8a6           |
| Azul   | #3b82f6             | #06b6d4           |
| Rosa   | #ec4899             | #f472b6           |
| Naranja| #f97316             | #fb923c           |

observations.title: Appearance Settings Sync - Design
observations.content:
# Design: Appearance Settings Sync

## Technical Approach

Implementar un EnhancedThemeProvider que:
1. Cargue settings desde API al montar (client-side)
2. Aplique colorTheme a CSS variables dinámicamente via inject en :root
3. Provea animations context que controle CSS global
4. Use forcedTheme de next-themes para prevenir flash

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Where to apply theme settings

**Choice**: Enhance ThemeProvider.tsx con lógica de sync
**Alternatives considered**: 
- Create new separate AppThemeProvider component
- Use Context API separately
- Route through layout.tsx props

**Rationale**: ThemeProvider ya existe y wraps next-themes. Extender es más limpio que crear otro provider. Mantiene la lógica de theme en un solo lugar.

### Decision: How to inject CSS variables

**Choice**: Create style tag in document head via useEffect
**Alternatives considered**:
- Modify globals.css at build time (no, requiere rebuild)
- Use CSS variables only in inline styles (no escala)

**Rationale**: React useEffect con document.head.append permite runtime sin rebuild. Simple y efectivo.

### Decision: How to handle animations toggle

**Choice**: CSS custom property --animation-duration
**Alternatives considered**:
- React Framer Motion (no usa animaciones)
- Toggle class on html element (más complejo)

**Rationale**: --animation-duration = "0s" desactiva todas las transiciones que usan esta variable, es lo más simple.

### Decision: How to prevent theme flash

**Choice**: Store theme preference in localStorage, set forcedTheme
**Alternatives considered**:
- Cookie-based (más complejo, same resultado)
- Server-side theme detection (no tenemos acceso a headers)

**Rationale**: localStorage es estándar en next-themes, forcedTheme evita el defaultTheme="system" que causa flash.

## Data Flow

```
API (/api/users/professors/settings)
        │
        ▼
EnhancedThemeProvider (useEffect on mount)
        │
        ├──▶ applyTheme(theme) ──▶ next-themes forcedTheme
        │
        ├──▶ applyColorTheme(colorTheme) ──▶ :root CSS variables
        │
        └──▶ applyAnimations(enabled) ──▶ --animation-duration
```

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `apps/frontend/src/components/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx` | Modify | Enhanced con sync logic, apply CSS variables |
| `apps/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` | Modify | Pasar attribute, enableSystem=false, agregar storage |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```typescript
// settings de API response
interface TeacherSettingsAPIResponse {
  theme: "light" | "dark";
  color_theme: "Indigo" | "Violeta" | "Esmeralda" | "Azul" | "Rosa" | "Naranja";
  animations_enabled: boolean;
}

// Color mapping
const COLOR_MAP: Record<string, { primary: string; accent: string } = {
  Indigo: { primary: "#6366f1", accent: "#8b5cf6" },
  Violeta: { primary: "#8b5cf6", accent: "#a855f7" },
  Esmeralda: { primary: "#10b981", accent: "#14b8a6" },
  Azul: { primary: "#3b82f6", accent: "#06b6d4" },
  Rosa: { primary: "#ec4899", accent: "#f472b6" },
  Naranja: { primary: "#f97316", accent: "#fb923c" },
};
```

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What to Test | Approach |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| Component | ThemeProvider applyColorTheme | Test unit con render + verify CSS |
| Integration | Settings page → ThemeProvider flow | Manual o E2E |
| E2E | Theme persists after refresh | Playwright |

## Migration / Rollout

1. No data migration needed (solo lectura)
2. Feature flag no necesario (es enhancement)
3. Deploy normal - backwards compatible

## Open Questions

- [ ] ¿Usar localStorage cache para evitar API call en cada mount?
  - Recomendación: Sí, guardar color_theme y animations_enabled en localStorage como fallback
- [ ] ¿Manejar caso cuando API fails?
  - Recomendación: Usar defaults (Indigo, animations=true) como fallback

## Next Steps (para tasks)

1. Modify ThemeProvider.tsx - agregar sync logic
2. Modify layout.tsx - configuración correcta
3. Test manual - verificar sincronización

observations.title: Appearance Settings Sync - Tasks
observations.content:
# Tasks: Appearance Settings Sync

## Phase 1: Foundation

- [ ] 1.1 Create COLOR_MAP constant in `apps/frontend/src/components/theme/theme-constants.ts` - exports Record mapping color names to hex values for primary and accent
- [ ] 1.2 Add type TeacherSettingsAPIResponse to `apps/frontend/src/components/theme/types.ts` - interface for API response with theme, color_theme, animations_enabled

## Phase 2: ThemeProvider Enhancement

- [ ] 2.1 Modify `apps/frontend/src/components/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx` - add useEffect to:
  - Fetch settings from /api/users/professors/settings
  - If color_theme exists, inject CSS variables into :root (--theme-primary, --theme-accent)
  - If animations_enabled=false, set --animation-duration: 0s
  - Store preferences in localStorage for flash prevention
- [ ] 2.2 Configure next-themes with forcedTheme prop - if localStorage has saved theme, use forcedTheme to prevent flash
- [ ] 2.3 Add theme sync hook in ThemeProvider - expose functions to update settings when user changes them in Settings page

## Phase 3: Integration

- [ ] 3.1 Modify `apps/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` - pass storageKey to ThemeProvider for localStorage persistence
- [ ] 3.2 Modify `apps/frontend/src/components/settings/settings-content.tsx` - when updating colorTheme or animationsEnabled, also apply to app (not just API)

## Phase 4: Testing

- [ ] 4.1 Manual test: Select "Esmeralda" theme → verify buttons use green (#10b981)
- [ ] 4.2 Manual test: Turn off animations → verify no transition delay
- [ ] 4.3 Manual test: Refresh page → no theme flash
- [ ] 4.4 E2E test: Settings persist after logout/login (Playwright)

## Phase 5: Cleanup

- [ ] 5.1 Remove hardcoded --theme-primary from `apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css` - use CSS variable fallback instead (currently #6366f1, should reference var(--theme-primary, #6366f1))
- [ ] 5.2 Add comments explaining CSS variable injection for future maintainers

observations.title: Implemented appearance settings sync
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented appearance settings sync for theme and color theme from API
**Why**: User requested to sync appearance settings from backend API to frontend theme system
**Where**: apps/frontend/src/components/theme/ThemeProvider.tsx, theme-constants.ts, types.ts, layout.tsx
**Learned**: next-themes forcedTheme prop prevents flash, need to use document.cookie parsing client-side since cookies() is server-only
